## Session #159 Summary (April 6, 2026)

### Purpose
Reconcile YSELA T0 decisions with Cultural Discipline Framework and plan Session 4 (S1E3) for stakeholder consensus.

### Key Corrections Made

| Item | Before (Wrong) | After (Correct) |
|------|---------------|-----------------|
| GRIT Definition | G-R-I-T signals/loop framework | Growth, Resilience, Integrity (character model) |
| Football Metaphor | Rigid language requirement | Example to illustrate deeper truths about excellence |
| Disciplines | Not defined | 9 Disciplines proposed (pending consensus) |
| Flow Model | Not defined | Formation → Behavior → Information → Decisions → Outcomes |

### Deliverables

| File | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| [YSELA/RESEARCH/README.md](YSELA/RESEARCH/README.md) | New | Research repository structure |
| [YSELA/RESEARCH/](YSELA/RESEARCH/) | New | 4 subfolders: white-papers, market-research, behavioral-science, case-studies |
| [S1E3_T0_FINALIZATION_PLAN.md](KARVIA_STRATEGY/3-DELIVERY/1-SPRINTS/SPRINT-21-Beta-Launch-Ready-In-Progress/S1E3_T0_FINALIZATION_PLAN.md) | New | Session 4 planning document |
| [YSELA_T0_DECISIONS.md](YSELA/YSELA_T0_DECISIONS.md) | Updated | GRIT corrected, Disciplines added, Flow Model added |

### Stakeholder Consensus Needed

Session 4 (S1E3) will address:
1. **GRIT Definition** - What do Growth, Resilience, Integrity mean?
2. **Disciplines** - Adopt 9 from Cultural Discipline, or create new?
3. **Flow Model** - Is Formation (small groups) required?
4. **Football Scope** - Example only, or primary language?

### Football as Excellence Example

Key insight from discussion: Football illustrates what makes teams excellent:
- Ball flows to best positioned, not most senior
- Standards set by group, not imposed
- Improvement is continuous, never "good enough"
- System survives individual departures

### Exit Criteria Met
- [x] GRIT corrected to Growth/Resilience/Integrity
- [x] RESEARCH folder structure created
- [x] S1E3 planning document created
- [x] YSELA_T0_DECISIONS.md updated with pending items
- [x] Football metaphor clarified as example

## Session #158 Summary (April 6, 2026)

### Purpose
Session 3 of T1 Remediation Plan - Establish fundamental YSELA identity through 6 key decisions.

### Decisions Made

| # | Question | Decision |
|---|----------|----------|
| Q1 | YSELA Identity | "An operating system that helps teams execute by making the right behaviors natural" |
| Q2 | Foundation | BBB (Behavior Based Business) is the core philosophy |
| Q3 | GRIT Definition | **Unified**: Both observation signals AND behavior loop |
| Q4 | Metaphors | Football Match metaphor CONFIRMED |
| Q5 | Principles | 4 non-negotiables: Behavior before goals, Team over individual, Consultant as coach, Evidence over intuition |
| Q6 | Boundaries | NOT: PM tool, reporting dashboard, self-serve SaaS, enterprise (>500) |

### Deliverables

| File | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| [YSELA_T0_DECISIONS.md](YSELA/YSELA_T0_DECISIONS.md) | New | T0 Constitutional document with all 6 decisions |

### GRIT Conflict Resolution

| Before | After |
|--------|-------|
| Definition A: Observation signals only | **UNIFIED MODEL** |
| Definition B: Behavior loop only | Signals show WHERE someone is |
| Confusion in docs | Loop shows WHAT to do next |

### Exit Criteria Met
- [x] All 6 questions answered
- [x] GRIT conflict resolved (unified model)
- [x] Football metaphor confirmed
- [x] 4 principles established

## Session #157 Summary (April 6, 2026)

### Purpose
Design comprehensive audit framework with MECE sub-audit commands and document cascade verification system.

### Deliverables

| File | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| [AUDIT_FRAMEWORK.md](.claude/AUDIT_FRAMEWORK.md) | New | Complete audit framework with 8 sub-audit specs |

### Framework Components

1. **Document Web Architecture**
   - T0: 4 docs (constitutional)
   - T1: 20 docs (strategic)
   - T2: 99 docs (canonical)
   - T3: 28 docs (working)
   - **Total: 151 genome-tagged documents**

2. **Sub-Audit Commands (MECE)**
   | Command | Focus | Standard |
   |---------|-------|----------|
   | `/audit-docs` | Tier cascade, cross-refs | Doc governance |
   | `/audit-security` | AuthN/Z, OWASP, multi-tenant | SOC 2, ISO 27001 |
   | `/audit-arch` | LEGO, scalability, API | TOGAF |
   | `/audit-data` | Models, privacy, GDPR | Data governance |
   | `/audit-ops` | Deploy, monitoring, backup | SRE, ITIL |
   | `/audit-ux` | WCAG, mobile, consistency | WCAG 2.1 AA |
   | `/audit-quality` | Tests, tech debt, metrics | ISO 25010 |
   | `/audit-compliance` | Regulatory, audit trail | Industry-specific |

3. **Cascade Verification System**
   - Parent→child relationship validation
   - Change impact analysis
   - Cross-reference integrity checks

4. **Team Access Patterns**
   - Strategy: T0, T1 vision docs
   - Product: T1-YSELA, T2 UX specs
   - Engineering: T1-KARVIA, T2 technical
   - QA: T2 contracts, T3 test specs

### Document Web Health

| Metric | Value | Status |
|--------|-------|--------|
| Genome coverage | 151 docs | Good |
| Stale docs (>30 days) | 2 | Acceptable |
| Draft status | 1 | Expected |
| Historical | 4 | Properly marked |

### Exit Criteria Met
- [x] Document web mapped with parent/child relationships
- [x] Team access patterns defined
- [x] Cascade verification system designed
- [x] All 8 sub-audit commands specified

## Sprint 18 V2.1 UX Enhancement Audit (March 10, 2026)

### Decision: Add M6 UX Enhancements (12 pts)

**New Total**: 42 pts (V2: 30 + M6: 12)

| Story | Points | Component |
|-------|--------|-----------|
| M6-1 | 3 | Slider: Employee Count |
| M6-2 | 2 | Chips: Industry Selection |
| M6-3 | 2 | Chips: Fee Structure |
| M6-4 | 2 | Hybrid Chips: Priority presets |
| M6-5 | 2 | Hybrid Chips: Blocker presets |
| M6-6 | 1 | Priority gold card visual |

### Adapter Pattern Approved

Preserve field IDs using hidden inputs:
- Chips → sync to hidden `<input type="hidden" id="originalFieldId">`
- Sliders → sync to hidden input on every change
- `collectFormData()` unchanged (reads hidden inputs)
- `populateForm()` extended for new components

### Documents Created
- SPRINT18_UX_ENHANCEMENT_AUDIT.md - Full adapter pattern + field mapping

### Deferred
- `industrySubtype` chips (complex cascading API)

## Sprint 18 Pre-Sprint Audit (March 10, 2026)

### Audit Findings Summary

**Dependencies**: ✅ ALL SATISFIED
- ContextMaturityService.js (430 lines)
- context-maturity.js routes (5 endpoints)
- maturity-indicator.js (dashboard component)
- Prompt System (9 files)
- BenchmarkProvider + OKROutcome model

**Risks Identified**:
| Risk | Level | Mitigation |
|------|-------|------------|
| Scope creep (71 pts) | HIGH | Prioritize CP1+CP6 first, defer CP5 if needed |
| JS preservation | MEDIUM | Surgical additions, test autosave after each session |
| CSS conflicts | MEDIUM | Namespace all new classes with `cp-` prefix |
| Tab migration | MEDIUM | Keep existing field IDs, wrap with stage containers |

**Recommended Execution Order**: CP6 → CP1 → CP2 → CP3 → CP4 → CP5

**Document Created**: `SPRINT18_RISK_AUDIT.md`

## Sprint 17 Phase 3 Session (March 9, 2026)

### Context Maturity System Complete (15 pts)

**MAT-1: ContextMaturityService (5 pts)**
- 13 weighted factors across 4 categories
- Profile (25%): description, business_model, value_proposition, strategic_priority
- Metrics (25%): baseline_metrics_count, has_targets
- Assessment (20%): ssi_completed, ssi_recent, twelve_block_coverage
- Historical (30%): okr_cycles_completed, task_history_depth, outcome_tracking, lessons_learned
- 5 stages: Discovery (0-20%) → Assessment (20-45%) → Execution (45-65%) → Learning (65-80%) → Mastery (80-100%)

**MAT-2: Maturity API Endpoints (3 pts)**
- GET /api/context-maturity - Full maturity calculation with gaps/strengths
- GET /api/context-maturity/quick - Lightweight stage check
- GET /api/context-maturity/stage/:n - Stage info lookup
- GET /api/context-maturity/stages - All stages with definitions
- GET /api/context-maturity/recommendations - Prioritized improvement actions

**MAT-3: Stage-Based Prompt Selection (4 pts)**
- `getPromptWithMaturity(companyId, promptType, context)` - Auto-detects maturity stage
- `getQuickMaturityStage(companyId)` - Fast stage lookup
- Lazy-loads ContextMaturityService to avoid circular dependencies
- Returns enriched prompt with guidance block and maturity metadata

**MAT-4: Dashboard Maturity Indicator (3 pts)**
- Reusable MaturityIndicator component in client/js/maturity-indicator.js
- Shows for EXECUTIVE, BUSINESS_OWNER, CONSULTANT roles
- Visual progress bar with stage colors
- Modal details view with breakdown and recommendations
- XSS protection with _escapeHtml()

### Files Created

- `client/js/maturity-indicator.js` (400 lines)

## Sprint 17 Planning Session (March 8, 2026)

### McKinsey-Style Progressive Context Building

**Problem Identified**: OKRs generated for KARVIA Consulting were generic with buzzwords, fabricated baselines, and no strategic alignment - despite having company profile data.

**Root Cause Analysis**:
1. Company profile data (25% utilization)
2. No baseline metrics (85% of companies have empty metrics.current)
3. No SSI assessment completion (40% completion rate)
4. No OKR outcome tracking (0% historical learning)
5. AI prompts don't adapt to context availability

### Research Completed

**McKinsey OKR Methodology**:
- 7-S Framework for organizational assessment
- Progressive engagement: credibility → context → confidence → capture
- Outcome-focused metrics over output metrics
- 3-3-3 framework (3 objectives, 3 KRs, 3 cycles)

**Data Audit Findings** (10 AI touchpoints across 5 services):
- AIContextService, aiOKRService, AIObjectivePlanner, SSINarrativeService, AIEstimator
- Strong task execution tracking (70%)
- Poor baseline metrics collection (15%)
- No outcome learning loop

### Sprint 17 Scope (89 pts / 3 weeks)

| Epic | Points | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| A: Context Maturity System | 21 | Stage 0-4 calculator, maturity API |
| B: Intelligent Data Collection | 18 | Guided profile, progressive disclosure |
| C: Adaptive AI Prompts | 24 | Stage-specific prompt templates |
| D: Industry Benchmark System | 13 | Benchmark data, fallback system |
| E: Outcome Tracking & Learning | 13 | OKR outcomes, lessons learned |

### Key Deliverables

1. **ContextMaturityService** - Calculate maturity stage (0-4) based on data completeness
2. **AdaptivePromptService** - Select stage-appropriate prompts
3. **OKROutcome model** - Track what worked/didn't work
4. **IndustryBenchmark model** - Industry templates for fallback
5. **Context Maturity API** - Endpoints for maturity assessment

### Files Created

```
KARVIA_STRATEGY/3-DELIVERY/1-SPRINTS/SPRINT-17 (Planned)/
├── SPRINT-17-MASTER-PLAN.md (comprehensive plan)
├── SPRINT17_HANDOFF_DOCUMENT.md (progress tracking)
└── SPRINT-17-TECHNICAL-IMPLEMENTATION.md (code specifications)
```

### Context Maturity Stages

| Stage | Score | Description | AI Behavior |
|-------|-------|-------------|-------------|
| 0 | 0-20% | Discovery | Industry benchmarks only |
| 1 | 20-45% | Assessment | Company profile + SSI |
| 2 | 45-65% | Execution | + Task patterns |
| 3 | 65-80% | Learning | + Outcome history |
| 4 | 80-100% | Mastery | Full predictive capability |

### Integration with iBrain (Future)

Sprint 17 establishes hooks for iBrain integration:
- `llm_context.maturity_stage` - Current maturity level
- `llm_context.outcome_history` - What worked/didn't
- `llm_context.pattern_library` - Company-specific patterns
- `llm_context.prediction_confidence` - AI confidence level

## Sprint 16-D Strategy Session (March 6, 2026)

### Documentation Living System - Complete Design (81 pts)

**Problem Identified**:
- 421 directories, only ~30 have READMEs (7% coverage)
- 761 markdown files with no metadata or versioning
- No central registry or dependency tracking
- Files disconnected from navigation hierarchy

**Solution Designed**:
1. **K0-K4 Tier System** - Document classification (Constitutional → Session)
2. **Doc ID Convention** - `K[TIER]-[DOMAIN]-[NUMBER]` pattern
3. **Metadata Headers** - Machine-readable DOC-META blocks
4. **README Network** - Every folder linked to hierarchy
5. **Document Registry** - Central JSON database
6. **Automation Tools** - Generator, validator, dependency checker

**Epic Breakdown** (81 pts):
| Epic | Points | Scope |
|------|--------|-------|
| D0: Foundation | 8 | Standards, templates |
| D1: Core READMEs | 13 | Top-level folders |
| D2: KARVIA_STRATEGY READMEs | 20 | Strategy subfolders |
| D3: Metadata Headers | 15 | K0-K2 documents |
| D4: Tooling & Registry | 15 | Automation scripts |
| D5: Remaining Folders | 10 | Auto-generation |

**Documents Created**:
- `SPRINT-16D-MASTER-PLAN.md` - Complete sprint specification
- `DOC_STANDARDS.md` - Master specification (K1-GUIDE-001)
- `README_TEMPLATE.md` - Copy-paste template (K1-GUIDE-002)

**Roadmap Updated**:
- Sprint 16-D added to product roadmap
- Sprint diagram updated with 81 pts allocation

## Bug Fix Session (February 21, 2026)

### ASSESSMENT QUESTIONS FIX COMPLETE ✅

**Issues Fixed:**

1. **Render Deployment (pre-prod)**
   - IPv6 rate limiting error (`ERR_ERL_KEY_GEN_IPV6`) - Added `validate: { ipKeyGenerator: false }`
   - Config shallow merge causing undefined HOST - Changed to lodash deep merge

2. **Assessment Question Semantics (173 questions fixed)**
   - 28 Financial Services questions had invalid `response_type: 'scale'` → Fixed to proper types
   - 32 questions changed from `perception` → `time_short`, `time_long`, `frequency`
   - 112 questions had labels updated (e.g., "Strongly Disagree" → "Not at all effective")
   - 28 additional questions with remaining semantic issues fixed

3. **API Bug - Labels Not Sent to Frontend**
   - `/api/assessments/:id/questions-anonymous` was stripping `labels` field
   - Fixed in `server/routes/assessments.js` - now includes labels, response_options, scale, weight, is_inverse

**Files Created:**
- `server/migrations/20260221-fix-financial-services-questions.js`
- `server/migrations/20260221-fix-question-semantics.js`
- `server/migrations/20260221-fix-remaining-semantics.js`
- `server/scripts/validateAssessmentQuestions.js`
- `server/scripts/auditQuestionSemantics.js`

**Branch:** pre-prod (pushed)

## Sprint 12 Module Evaluation Complete (January 9, 2026)

### Session: Cohesive Data Flow Analysis & Sprint 12 Planning (Jan 9 - Latest)

**Strategy Session Completed:**
1. ✅ Analyzed 4 core modules as "lego blocks" (Assessment, Company Profile, OKR Creation, Planning/Dashboard)
2. ✅ Mapped complete data flow identifying disconnections
3. ✅ Identified CRITICAL gap: OKR targets are generic hardcoded values (30%, 20%, 15%)
4. ✅ Created Sprint 12 Master Plan (48 pts) to build cohesive system
5. ✅ Created Before/After data flow visualization document

**Key Finding - The Problem:**
```
Current: Assessment → Generic SSI → AI → "Improve retention by 30%" (ALWAYS 30%!)
Target:  Assessment → 12-Block Detail → Business Metrics → "Improve from 72% to 85%"
```

**Root Causes Identified:**
1. 12-block SSI scores calculated but NOT persisted (only aggregates saved)
2. No baseline values captured (current_value always 0)
3. KR target templates hardcoded (not derived from company data)
4. Industry enum mismatch between Company model and industries.js

**Sprint 12 Epics (48 pts):**

| Epic | Points | Focus |
|------|--------|-------|
| Epic N | 21 | Detailed SSI Block Persistence & Targeting |
| Epic O | 18 | Smart KR Target Calculator |
| Epic P | 9 | Company Profile Industry Alignment |

**Epic N Stories (21 pts):**
- N1: Assessment model extension - detailed_block_scores field (5 pts)
- N2: UnifiedSSIScoringService update - save blocks after assessment (3 pts)
- N3: SSI Diagnostic Report UI - 12-block visual breakdown (5 pts)
- N4: Company model - last_assessment_blocks reference (3 pts)
- N5: AIContextService - include weak blocks in OKR context (5 pts)

**Epic O Stories (18 pts):**
- O1: TargetCalculatorService - core calculation logic (5 pts)
- O2: Industry benchmark data structure (3 pts)
- O3: AI prompt enhancement - pass baseline + benchmarks (5 pts)
- O4: KR model extension - baseline_value field (3 pts)
- O5: Frontend display - "from X to Y" format (2 pts)

**Epic P Stories (9 pts):**
- P1: Company model - use industries.js enum (3 pts)
- P2: Add industry_subtype field (3 pts)
- P3: Company Profile UI - dynamic industry dropdowns (3 pts)

**Files Created:**
- `SPRINT-12 (Planned)/SPRINT-12-MASTER-PLAN.md` - Full sprint specification
- `SPRINT-12 (Planned)/DATA-FLOW-BEFORE-AFTER.md` - Visual data flow comparison

**Data Flow After Sprint 12:**
```
Assessment (12 blocks persisted)
    → Company Profile (baseline metrics + industry subtype)
        → TargetCalculatorService (exact numbers)
            → AI OKR Generation ("from 72% to 85%")
                → Planning (existing cascade works)
                    → Dashboard (progress tracking)
```

**Next**: Execute Sprint 10 → Sprint 11 → Sprint 12 in sequence

### Session: Sprint 10 Planning v2 & Epic J Design (Jan 9 - Previous)

**Strategy Session Completed:**
1. ✅ Identified Assessment Credibility issues from audit findings
2. ✅ Designed Modular Question System (Core/Industry/Role)
3. ✅ Designed 3-step Template Creation flow with left-right layout
4. ✅ Completed thorough impact analysis (schema, API, frontend)
5. ✅ Created SPRINT-10-MASTER-PLAN.md with 116 pts total

**Audit Findings Addressed:**
- 12-block scoring can be skewed (templates only require ≥10 questions)
- Role-based questions not functional
- Mixed taxonomies between legacy and MECE
- No industry context in generic questions

**Solution: Modular Question System**
- **Core Module**: 24 questions (2 per block × 12 blocks) - ensures coverage
- **Industry Module**: 36 questions (6 industries × 6 questions each)
- **Role Module**: 20 questions (5 roles × 4 questions each)
- Questions NOT locked - flexible selection

**3-Step Template Creation Flow:**
- Step 1: Select Questions (left-right layout with module tabs)
- Step 2: Configure Template (name, description, weights, visibility)
- Step 3: Review & Save (summary, coverage check, preview)

**Key Design Decisions:**
- Core questions NOT locked - keep flexible
- Response scales: Quantitative (percentage ranges), Qualitative (maturity scale)
- Existing templates unchanged (backward compatible)
- Auto-migrate existing questions to `legacy` module

**Epic J Stories (31 pts):**
- J1: Module System Database Schema (5 pts)
- J2: Core Questions Seed (3 pts)
- J3: Industry Questions Seed (3 pts)
- J4: Role Questions Seed (2 pts)
- J5: Module-Filtered Questions API (5 pts)
- J6: Step 1 - Select Questions Page (8 pts)
- J7: Step 2 - Configure Template Page (3 pts)
- J8: Step 3 - Review & Save Page (2 pts)

**Impact Analysis Summary:**
- Schema changes: 4 new fields on AssessmentQuestion model
- API changes: Enhanced question filtering, template creation
- Frontend: 3 new pages, 1 new JS module, redesigned question library
- Migration: Auto-migrate existing questions to 'legacy' module

## Key Decisions & Learnings

### Session 1 (Strategy - Nov 23)
**Decisions**:
- Chose Epic-based approach over strict day-by-day
- Prioritized critical gaps (Epic 2 & 4) first
- Established session break points for token management

**Learnings**:
- Comprehensive planning saves coding time
- Clear session types improve focus
- Handoff documents critical for continuity

### Session 2 (Coding - Nov 23)
**Decisions**:
- Implemented Epic 1 (Date System) first
- Fixed critical audit issues immediately
- Created SESSION_BREAK_NOTES.md at 55% token usage

**Learnings**:
- DateService pattern works well for fiscal years
- ValidationService prevents bugs early
- Proactive session breaks maintain quality

### Session 3 (General - Nov 23)
**Decisions**:
- Created comprehensive strategy framework
- Defined 5 session types with clear metrics
- Established quality gates and review processes

**Learnings**:
- Structure improves Claude effectiveness
- Metrics enable continuous improvement
- Documentation as code principle essential

## Sprint 18 Strategy Session (March 9, 2026)

### Sprint Focus: AI-Ready Company Profile

**Problem Identified**: Sprint 17 built intelligent context engine, but users aren't providing the data AI needs. Profile completion rates are low (~40%).

**Solution**: Make profile completion *visible, valuable, and easy*.

### 4 Epics Defined (55 pts total)

| Epic | Points | Focus |
|------|--------|-------|
| CP1: Maturity Visibility | 15 | Show users their AI context stage |
| CP2: Guided Completion | 18 | AI impact badges, tooltips, suggestions |
| CP3: Metrics Enhancement | 12 | Quick input, YoY tracking, gap analysis |
| CP4: Lessons Integration | 10 | Surface learnings from outcome tracking |

### Key Insight

> "Work backwards from what AI needs to generate excellent OKRs"

The Context Maturity Service (Sprint 17) weights:
- Strategic Priority: 10% (often empty)
- Baseline Metrics: 15% (often empty)
- Company Description: 5% (often empty)

**Making these visible on the Company Profile page will drive completion.**

### Documents Created
1. SPRINT18_MASTER_PLAN.md - Full sprint specification
2. SPRINT18_HANDOFF_DOCUMENT.md - Ready for coding session

### Recommended First Coding Task
**Epic CP1-1**: Add maturity stage indicator to Company Profile header
- Reuse `maturity-indicator.js` from Sprint 17
- Quick win showing immediate value
- 5 story points

## Sprint 16D Strategic Update & Critical Analysis — March 19, 2026

### Session Info
| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Type | Strategy |
| Duration | ~1.5h |
| Tokens | ~50K |
| Quality | 9/10 |

### Objectives
1. Update Sprint 16D with new epics for command management
2. Design document relevance classification system (R0-R3)
3. Create local archive strategy for completed sprints
4. Critical analysis of Sprint 16D value proposition
5. Address P1/P2 findings from review

### Completed Work

| Task | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Sprint 16D expansion | ✅ | Added D7, D8, D9 epics (+46 pts) |
| Critical analysis | ✅ | Full vs Lean comparison |
| P1/P2 findings addressed | ✅ | 4 P1, 3 P2 issues documented |
| Optimization report | ✅ | Created with recommendations |
| Sprint put on hold | ✅ | Folder renamed to "On Hold" |

### P1 Findings (Critical)

| Finding | Resolution |
|---------|------------|
| P1-1: Scope contradiction | Sprint mislabeled as "documentation-only" |
| P1-2: .local/ inadvisable | **REJECTED** - institutional memory > 2.3MB |
| P1-3: Folder merge under-analyzed | Needs dependency inventory first |
| P1-4: Stale baseline data | Must update metrics before planning |

### P2 Findings (Important)

| Finding | Resolution |
|---------|------------|
| P2-1: Activity metrics | Replace with outcome metrics |
| P2-2: Duplicates governance | Audit existing before adding |
| P2-3: Lean still had .local/ | Removed from all recommendations |

### Final Decision

**Sprint 16D: ON HOLD**

| Option | Verdict |
|--------|---------|
| Full 16D (147 pts) | REJECT |
| Lean 16D (38 pts) | REJECT |
| Focus on Sprint 20 | RECOMMENDED |

### Files Created/Modified
- `SPRINT-16D-MASTER-PLAN.md` - Added epics, updated status to ON HOLD
- `SPRINT16D_OPTIMIZATION_REPORT.md` - Created with P1/P2 analysis
- Folder renamed: `(Skipped)` → `(Planned)` → `(On Hold)`

### Key Learnings
1. Volume ≠ Value (421 READMEs doesn't prove better DX)
2. Institutional memory matters more than storage savings
3. Path merges are dependency migrations, not cosmetic renames
4. Activity metrics don't prove outcomes

### Next Session Recommendation
**Type**: `/coding`
**Focus**: Sprint 20 OW-IMPACT (5 pts) - Objective Wizard impact analysis
**Priority**: P1

### Session Status: ✅ COMPLETE

## GRIT-UX Principles Session (March 24, 2026)

### Session Summary
Created comprehensive **YSELA UX Principles** document establishing GRIT-UX framework for intuitive adoption. Added 20 story points across Sprints 21-25.

### UX Gaps Identified & Addressed

| Gap | Solution |
|-----|----------|
| No onboarding journey | FTUE 3-step flow (UX-001) |
| No empty states | Empty state library (UX-002, UX-009) |
| No progressive disclosure | Feature unlock system (UX-003) |
| No contextual help | Hint system (UX-004) |
| No celebration patterns | Toast + celebration hierarchy (UX-005, UX-006) |
| No personalization | Preference storage (UX-007) |

### GRIT-UX Framework

```
TRIGGER    →  GROWTH      →  REINFORCE   →  INVESTMENT
(Entry)       (Learning)     (Feedback)     (Ownership)
```

### Core Principle Established

> "If a user needs training to use YSELA, we've failed. The UX itself must be the teacher."

| Version | Points | Key Change | Status |

| V1 | 55 | Original plan | Superseded |

| V3 | 40 | Zero-load approach | Good but incomplete |
| V4 | 48 | Hardened + /deploy + /quick-fix | **APPROVED** |

| Rejected | Adopted |
|----------|---------|
| DOC_ID_REGISTRY.md file | Computed registry via grep |

| 5 | Command aliases | Lost discoverability |
| 6 | Token budget tracking | Context overflow |

| 8 | Sequence suggestion | ID collisions |
| 9 | Scale threshold docs | Future perf issues |

| 191b–h | May 1 | Coding (Defect cluster) | ~5h | ~180K | 0 (defect fixes on shipped #191 scope) | 10/10 | Planning page simplification + 7 cascading bug fixes | User-driven simplification of #191's Epic H planning page after live verification revealed the AI-Presets panel + 12-13-week scaffold confused users (clicking "Generate Weekly Goals" appeared to do nothing because the scaffold already showed empty week placeholders). 7 commits chained as #191b → #191h. **#191b** (95640ae): three-state model — empty CTA / extend / generating; dropped AI-Presets panel + scaffold + month-grouped 13-week pre-render. **#191c** (b679565): /api/weekly-goals/:krId 404 fix — added embedded-KR fallback in `projectLegacyGoal` for pre-#190 objectives whose KRs only live in `Objective.key_results[]` (no standalone collection row). **#191d** (e2ddbc5): `legacyWeekToISO` ISO-year bug — was returning `weekMonday.getFullYear()` (calendar year of Monday) instead of `d.getUTCFullYear()` (year of Thursday). For Q1W1-2026, Mon Dec 29 2025's calendar year is 2025 but ISO year is 2026; pre-fix surfaced "December 2024" group, post-fix correctly under January 2026. **#191e** (243ddb8): Extend Plan wired to `/api/planning/extend` (was hitting `/generate-weekly-plan` which 409s on existing goals). Smart prefill = Monday after latest existing week; modal label flips to "Extend Plan"; client-side validation checks objective bounds + 1..13 quarter cap + overlap; new helpers `parseDateAsLocal` (TZ-safe), `dateToLegacyWeekOfQuarter`, `validateExtendRange`. **#191f** (86a46a3): robust response handling — `parseResponseSafely` reads body as text first, branches on content-type/response.ok before JSON.parse, surfaces "Server error 502 — generation took too long" instead of the misleading "JSON.parse: unexpected character" toast users saw on Render gateway timeouts. **#191g** (4eaf3f4): bulk-insert weekly plan generation — replaced sequential `await goal.save()` + `await task.save()` with `Promise.all([Goal.insertMany, Task.insertMany, quarterlyGoal.save()])` + single `Objective.updateOne` aggregate. Pre-fix: ~96 DB round-trips through Goal/Task post-save cascade middleware = 30-45s on Render-dev = 502 gateway. Post-fix: ~3 RTs = <3s. Includes graceful VersionError/DocumentNotFoundError handling for parallel-delete races. **#191h** (81bddbb): `goal.start_date` is authoritative — `projectLegacyGoal` now derives `target_year`/`target_week` from `goal.start_date` (the actual calendar Monday) instead of from `(year, quarter, week)` index tuple. Frontend month grouping switched from Monday-anchored to Thursday-anchored (ISO 8601 convention) so all of Q1's weeks fall under Jan/Feb/Mar even though W1's Monday is in Dec. Tests: scripts/test-sprint23-191-planning-page.js — 71 → **135 assertions green** (cumulative +64 across the chain incl. ISO-year boundary, embedded-KR fallback, extend round-trip, /generate happy-path with <5s wall-time bound). S22a regression: 184a (55) + 184c (30) + 184e (36) all still green. Filed DEBT-006 (Date-Sync Across OKR Cascade, P1, 13-19 pts). Logged Apr/May visual edge case (start_date=Fri May 1 2026 ISO-snapped under Apr 27 Thursday-anchored) to backlog as part of DEBT-006. **All commits pushed to development** (Render dev auto-deployed). |
| 192-prep + 192a | May 1–2 | Strategy + Coding | ~6h | ~210K | 3 (#192a) | 10/10 | Refined Epic G doc + Navy theme + Tasks→Moves rename + display label constants | **§192-prep** (9 commits, doc-only): refined Epic G scoping doc at `KARVIA_STRATEGY/3-DELIVERY/1-SPRINTS/SPRINT-23/epics/EPIC_G_DASHBOARD_UI_REFINED.md`. Walked Q1–Q10 + Q3b with user, recording every decision inline as commits accumulated. Key reframings captured: (1) dashboard is **role-agnostic** & **continuous** (planning/execution/reflection are not weekly-sliced or role-sliced — football-match metaphor: even consultant is a player); (2) **5-layer cascade with explicit Task→Move handoff** (KARVIA owns layers 1–4 — Objective→KR→Plan→Tasks; YSELA owns layer 5 — filter/reframe/distribute as Moves); (3) **BBB is a hypothesis being tested, not delivered** — every dashboard element ships as "v1 proposal — replaceable"; (4) **§10a Hardline Modularity** (BINDING) — every "twist" lives in a single named function in `MoveDashboardService`, no new required schema fields, no coupling between sections. Decisions locked: Q1 Behavior tab = regrouping by `Objective.discipline_ids[]` (primary = first-in-array, Q1b.2.i); Q2 = 4 foundations (Discipline/Growth/Accountability/Maturity) as v1 starter; Q3 = system-term status display (`not_started/in_progress/done/removed/overdue` + `on_track/at_risk/behind`); Q3b = 6-stage Objective lifecycle view-time derivation (`identified→kr_breakdown→in_execution→completion_review→completed→sustained_mode`) with `sustained_mode` = "behaviors-as-culture" semantic; Q4 = pushed-tile derived purely from `created_at`+`due_date`+`updated_at` (no persistent counting); Q5 = right-column shows Objective progress + KR via `Move→WeeklyGoal→KR` chain (NOT KR lift over time as mockup suggested) + new optional `Objective.short_label` field (the only §10a-permitted exception); Q6 = Option Now Move classification — LLM piggybacks on per-KR Task generation prompt, returns `{move_doc | null}` per Task in `MoveClassifierService.classifyTaskFromAIResponse()`; Q7 = pure role-agnostic consultant dashboard (consultant is a player, my-clients.html stays separate); Q8 = single 720px breakpoint; Q9 = light telemetry scaffold via logger sink (`TelemetryService.emit()` ready for Phase 1 swap to HTTP webhook); Q10 = universal action set with Complete↔Re-open toggle. Filed 4 new backlog items: DEBT-007 (Objective Lifecycle Redesign, P1, 15-25 pts), DEBT-008 (Behavioral Telemetry Postpone Tracking, P2, 5-8 pts), DEBT-009 (Behavioral Classification Research, P2, TBD), and earlier DEBT-006 (Date-Sync). All 16 §14 sign-off items closed at commit 490afbe. **§192a implementation** (commit 83fdaba): Navy theme alias — `objective-wizard.css:13` `--karvia-primary` flipped from purple `#7C3AED` to navy `#1e3a5f`; `s13-patterns.css` flipped as defensive baseline. Dashboard purple sweep — 13 inline `#8B5CF6` references + 2 Tailwind `bg-purple-100`/`text-purple-600` classes → navy equivalents; CSS class `.progress.purple` renamed to `.progress.navy`. Tasks→Moves user-visible rename — 8 HTML strings (section heading, 4 modal titles, edit form label, empty-state heading) + 14 toast messages + 2 title attrs swept; JS identifiers (`taskId`, `taskList`, `pendingPostponeTaskId`), CSS class names (`.task-row`, `.task-stat`), and API endpoints (`/api/tasks/*`) preserved unchanged per Q4-followup. Display label constants added to `dashboard-v2.js` — `MOVE_TYPE_LABEL` (`habit: 'Behavior'`), `MOVE_STATUS_LABEL` (system terms), `HEALTH_STATUS_LABEL` (system terms), `OBJECTIVE_LIFECYCLE_LABEL` (6-stage). NEW `scripts/test-sprint23-192a-theme-rename.js` — **52/52 green** (theme alias [9] + display rename [14] + label constants [12 + 5 sandboxed runtime] + preservation regression [12]). S22a regression: 184a (55) + 184c (30) + 184e (36) + S23 #191 (135) all still green. **All 12 commits pushed to development** (`81bddbb..83fdaba`). **Sprint 23 Progress: 26/33 (79%)** — #192a complete, #192b (V3 layout body + Move classification at generation + dashboard-summary endpoint + telemetry scaffold + responsive, ~7 pts) outstanding. **PAUSED** for user to verify #192a deployed on `karvia-business-1.onrender.com` before stacking #192b on top. |
| 192b | May 4 | Coding | ~3h | ~135K | 7 (Epic G Phase B) | 10/10 | Dashboard V3 + MoveDashboardService seams + MoveClassifierService + TelemetryService + LLM prompt extension + planning bulk-insert Moves + dashboard-summary aggregator + V3 HTML/JS + 720px responsive | **Backend (read-only, additive, §10a-modular)**: NEW `server/services/MoveDashboardService.js` — single-fn seams: `bucketizeMove` (Q4 pure derivation from created_at/due_date/updated_at), `deriveMoveFoundations` (Q1b.2.i first-in-array primary), `deriveObjectiveLifecycle` (Q3b 6 stages + paused/cancelled edges), `deriveRelevantKR` (Q5.x.A soft-fail with "Unassigned KR" placeholder), `deriveObjectiveSummary` (Q5.2.ii short_label fallback to truncated title), `computeMomentum` (up/down/flat from period-over-period count), `computeKRLift` (placeholder until DEBT-007 snapshot system), `resolveFoundationLabel`, `listFoundations`. NEW `server/services/MoveClassifierService.js` — single export `classifyTaskFromAIResponse(taskResponse)` returning `(Move-shape POJO \| null)` per Q6. Returns null on `is_chore=true / move_type=null / unknown move_type / null input`; defaults frequency by move_type (`habit→daily`, others→`once`); silently drops invalid disciplines; carries discipline/priority/title from LLM response; Q4 hides AI-vs-user provenance from current UI (decision deferred to S24 hybrid epic). NEW `server/services/TelemetryService.js` — Q9.B log-sink `emit(event, payload)` writing `[telemetry]` prefix via `logger.info`; never throws (best-effort); single sink — Phase 1 swaps to HTTP webhook in one function edit. NEW route `GET /api/moves/dashboard-summary` in `server/routes/moves.js` — single-RT aggregator, route handler ASSEMBLES the join (Move → parent goal lookup in legacy Goal OR new WeeklyGoal collection → Objective via FK → embedded KR via `key_results.id()` OR populate) and DELEGATES every twist to MoveDashboardService; tenant-scoped via `req.user.company_id`; soft-fails orphan Moves with placeholder; returns `{ kpi, bucket_counts, moves[], chores[], foundations, empty_state_hints }`; emits `dashboard.viewed` telemetry event. `PUT /api/moves/:id` extended to capture pre-state (status/due_date/assigned_to) and emit `move.completed` / `move.postponed` / `move.reassigned` per state-changing diff (no false emits on no-ops). `server/routes/planning.js` `/generate-weekly-plan` + `/extend` — LLM prompt schema extended with per-Task `move_type / frequency / discipline / is_chore` fields + classification rules in system prompt; classifier piggybacks; non-null classifications produce Move POJO seeds enriched with `{company_id, weekly_goal_id=Goal._id, assigned_to, created_by, due_date, status='todo', ai_generated=true}` and bulk-inserted via `Move.insertMany` alongside `Goal.insertMany`/`Task.insertMany` in the existing `Promise.all` (extends #191g atomic-bulk pattern). `server/models/Objective.js` — added optional `short_label: String, default: ''` (Q5.2.ii §10a-permitted exception, no forced migration). **Frontend (V3 single-fetch render)**: `client/pages/dashboard-v2.html` — replaced two old `<section>` blocks (Tasks 3-col + Weekly Goals 3-col) with V3 layout: KPI strip (`X moves · Y% this week · ▲`), group switch (`All / Objective / Behavior` per Q1), catch-up tiles (`pushed / forgotten / this week` per Q3), causal-chain cards (left=Building / right=Moving per Q5), chores list (§6.5), empty states (§6.6 data-driven CTA routing). Single `@media (max-width: 720px)` block (Q8.A): KPI flex-stacks, group-switch full-width, catch-up tiles single-column, card columns vertical with arrow rotated 90°, action chips always visible. Nav block + script tags + 4 modals (postpone/reassign/edit/delete) preserved verbatim. `client/pages/scripts/dashboard-v2.js` — full V3 rewrite (1456→ ~620 LOC). Single fetch to `/api/moves/dashboard-summary` orchestrating per-section renderers (`renderKPI` / `renderCatchUpTiles` / `renderMovesGroups` / `renderEmptyState` / `renderChores` / `renderCausalChainCard`). Q10.B universal action set with Complete↔Re-open toggle. Display label constants (`MOVE_TYPE_LABEL` / `MOVE_STATUS_LABEL` / `HEALTH_STATUS_LABEL` / `OBJECTIVE_LIFECYCLE_LABEL`) preserved as single-seam swap point for Q3 voice (deferred per Q3.B). NEW `scripts/test-sprint23-192b-dashboard-v3.js` — **128/128 green** initially, **134** after BUG-01, **136** after BUG-02. Pure-fn (40) + classifier (13) + telemetry (3) + frontend wiring (28) + §10a invariants (15) + live aggregator (20) + telemetry emission (6) + cross-tenant guard + orphan soft-fail. **Full regression at session end**: 16 suites, **803 assertions, all green** (S22a 215 + Phase 107 + S23 481). **§10a invariants verified**: zero executable `switch-company` callers in `client/pages/scripts/` (test-asserted); LLMGateway sole executable OpenAI chokepoint (comment-stripped grep verifies); StageTransitionService hooks present in objectives.js / assessments.js / invitations.js / objective-wizard.js; Move enum unchanged `[action,reaction,habit]`; Task model untouched (no `is_chore` / `move_type` / any new field). **Commit `2530cf2` pushed to development.** Sprint 23 Progress: 33/33 (100%). |

KR-aggregation cron). Refinement-track work (owner-side redesign, display-labels owner mapping, behavior persistence β, prompt tuning, behavior-taxonomy lock) deferred to post-S27. **Folder restructure executed**: `SPRINT-25-Plumbing/` + `SPRINT-26-First-Objective/` + `SPRINT-27-First-Task/` + `REFINEMENT-BACKLOG/` (with 3 deferred drafts moved here); old SPRINT-25 master plan rewritten as SPRINT-26 master plan (theme replaced); cascade epics 25-1 + 25-2 moved to S25-Plumbing/epics. **8 new docs created**: 3 master plans (T3-SPR-{025,026,027}-MP) + 3 handoff docs + SPRINT_X_VERIFICATION.md scaffold (T2-TECH-SPR25-VERIFY at `KARVIA_STRATEGY/2-TECHNICAL/`) + REFINEMENT-BACKLOG/README.md. **4 cross-refs in S24 docs updated** to reflect new 3-sprint sequence. **Phase 6 — cross-sprint architectural audit pre-work**: launched 3 parallel Explore passes (Pass A CTO/Technical 9 dimensions T1-T9, Pass B CPO/Product 7 dimensions P1-P7, Pass C Cross-sprint Seams 4 boundaries α/β/γ/δ). **Findings: 56 total, 13 Critical, 22 High, 14 Medium, 7 Low**. Critical findings consolidate into **8 decision groups** (many findings surface same root cause from different lenses): Group 1 S24 Acceptance Gate Enforcement (T1-1 + C-α-1), Group 2 Dispatcher Architecture Specification (T4-1 + C-β-1 + C-γ-1 + P5-1; needs DISPATCHER_CONTRACT.md), Group 3 Activation Surface Design (P6-1 YSELA philosophy break + P2-1 cohort parity gap + P3-1 empty-state copy), Group 4 Persona Coverage (P1-1 Executive + P1-2 Employee), Group 5 Notification Design (P4-1 + T9-1 + P4-2; needs NOTIFICATION_STRATEGY.md), Group 6 Schema Migration Ordering (T3-1 + T3-2), Group 7 BallState/LifecycleTransition Scope (T2-1 + M-α-5), Group 8 Beta Launch Readiness (C-δ-1 + P7-1; needs BETA_LAUNCH_CHECKLIST.md). **Three systemic risks surfaced**: (1) contracts are verbal not written, (2) acceptance is sprint-local not cross-seam, (3) refinement-track is shadow track without timing. **AUDIT_S24_S27_FINDINGS.md created** at `KARVIA_STRATEGY/3-DELIVERY/1-SPRINTS/` consolidating all 56 findings into 8 walkable groups + sign-off matrices for CTO + CPO + re-audit trigger conditions. **Phase 7 — handoff decision**: user asked which path minimizes hallucination for the actual sign-off walkthrough; honest assessment: Option B (fresh `/audit` session with findings doc as auto-load context) — lowest hallucination risk because conversation has accumulated ~190K tokens including 7 sub-agent reports I've only seen as summaries; fresh context allows clean code re-reads when drafting DISPATCHER_CONTRACT/NOTIFICATION_STRATEGY/BETA_LAUNCH_CHECKLIST artifacts. **Quality reflection**: 9/10 self-rating — strategic alignment achieved, comprehensive doc artifacts produced, honest about own limits at end. Negative: required 4-5 user corrections to reach right framing (assessment-as-stage, LLM-as-co-author both stages, "everything is built", "stitch leaks first"). Each correction drove better outcome but suggests I should front-load skepticism on my initial framings, especially when carrying cached priors from typical OKR-tool architectures. Original plan would force stub-then-rewrite. (4) **TRANSITIONS table mismatch** — `objectiveLifecycle.js` had strict `completion_review → completed → sustained_mode` chain, but Epic 24.3 spec wants direct `completion_review → sustained_mode` (one-click "Mark Sustained" UX) plus `→ in_execution` re-open. Surfaced all to user with 3 options (reorder / push through with stubs / strategy patch first); user chose reorder + "see if missing anything else also quickly". **Phase B — locked plan with user**: new sequence B=24.3-P1 (this session) → C=24.1 + 24.8 B-8 ride-along → D=24.2 + 24.8 B-6 ride-along → E=24.9 → F=24.3-P2 → G=24.4 → H=24.5 + Sprint close. Handoff Re-order block 2026-05-06 added. **Phase C — implementation (12 todos)**: (1) [server/constants/objectiveLifecycle.js](server/constants/objectiveLifecycle.js) — `TRANSITIONS.completion_review` now `['sustained_mode', 'in_execution', 'completed']`. (2) [server/models/Objective.js](server/models/Objective.js) — `consultant_notes` (`String`, `default: ''`, `maxlength: 280`, `select: false` → R7 schema-level privacy); `sustained_eligible` virtual (`key_results.length > 0 && every(kr.status === 'completed')`). (3) NEW [server/services/LifecycleTransitionService.js](server/services/LifecycleTransitionService.js) — generic `createInstance({Model, stageField, historyField, TRANSITIONS, STAGES, serviceName})` factory; two configured instances exported (`companyStageInstance`, `objectiveLifecycleInstance`); methods: `transitionTo` (idempotent flip via `findOneAndUpdate({_id, [stageField]: fromState})`), `manualTransition` (validated against catalog), `manualOverride` (catalog-bypass tagged `triggered_by_kind='manual_override'`), `appendHistoryOnly` (sentinel `to:'__event__'`); telemetry: every successful flip emits `lifecycle.transition` via existing `TelemetryService` (#192b) with `{service, doc_id, from, to, actor, triggered_by_id, triggered_by_kind, has_note}`; objective-only composites: `evaluateAndTransitionAfterWrite` (predicate ladder) + `markSustained` (eligibility-guarded); convenience `fireAfterWriteEvaluator({res, objectiveId, ...})` for routes — wraps `res.on('finish')` with status-code gate (skip on 4xx/5xx), supports lazy ID resolution. (4) [server/services/StageTransitionService.js](server/services/StageTransitionService.js) — refactored to thin wrapper; 4 public methods preserved verbatim with original return shape `{flipped, history_added, company}` for 184e contract; `consultant:<userId>` actor format for `manualTransition` retained literal; **retargeted `onFirstObjectiveCreated` `'objective_identified'` → `'active'`** per Epic 24.1 spec lock. (5) [server/models/Company.js](server/models/Company.js) — `stage.enum` collapsed from `ALL_STAGES_TRANSITIONAL` (10) to canonical `STAGES` (6); legacy values now reject. (6) NEW [client/js/display-labels.js](client/js/display-labels.js) — `window.DisplayLabels` IIFE; subsumes S23 #192a inline label constants from dashboard-v2.js (`MOVE_TYPE_LABEL`, `MOVE_STATUS_LABEL`, `HEALTH_STATUS_LABEL`, `OBJECTIVE_LIFECYCLE_LABEL`); adds `COMPANY_STAGE_LABEL` (canonical 6) + `COMPANY_STAGE_ICON` + `CONSULTANT_BALL_VIEW` (3-bucket); `companyStageView(stage)` with safe fallback; `lifecycleView(stage, role)` role-aware — consultant returns ball-view bucket+icon, owner/manager/employee return canonical placeholder until S25 Epic 25.4. CommonJS-export-compatible for Node test consumption. (7) **5 `res.on('finish')` evaluator hooks** wired via `fireAfterWriteEvaluator`: [routes/objectives.js](server/routes/objectives.js) POST + PUT, [routes/key-results.js](server/routes/key-results.js) POST + PUT, [routes/weekly-goals.js](server/routes/weekly-goals.js) POST (extended `kr` select to include `objective_id`), [routes/moves.js](server/routes/moves.js) POST (lazy WG → KR → Objective lookup), [routes/objective-wizard.js](server/routes/objective-wizard.js) finalize + retargeted log message `→ active`. Predicate ladder: `identified + has any KR (embedded OR collection) → kr_breakdown` → `kr_breakdown + WG (WeeklyGoal collection) + owner ∈ {BO, EXECUTIVE, MANAGER, EMPLOYEE} → in_execution` → `in_execution + every KR status='completed' → completion_review`. Idempotent: each rung uses from-stage filter so calling N times = calling once. `sustained_mode` is manual-only via `markSustained`. (8) [routes/consultant.js](server/routes/consultant.js) — 3 new endpoints under `consultantClientGuards`: `PATCH .../objectives/:oid/notes` (validates 280-char), `POST .../mark-sustained` (calls `markSustained` — 409 on `not_sustained_eligible`), `POST .../override-state` (calls `manualOverride`). Helper `loadManagedObjective(req, res)` validates ObjectId + tenant + selects `+consultant_notes` only on consultant code path. (9) **Privacy at schema level** — `consultant_notes: select: false` means default reads omit it; static-grep test asserts no owner-side route in `routes/objectives.js` opts in via `+consultant_notes`. (10) NEW [scripts/test-sprint24-243-objectives-and-lifecycle.js](scripts/test-sprint24-243-objectives-and-lifecycle.js) — 125 in-memory mongo assertions across 15 sections: constants shape (9), schema additions + virtuals (10), idempotency + telemetry (8), manualTransition catalog (3), manualOverride bypass (2), predicate ladder including consultant-owner-blocks-in_execution (10), markSustained guard (5), 184e wrapper regression including S24 retarget assertion (10), Company.stage enum collapse (10 — 4 legacy reject + 6 canonical accept), display-labels.js coverage (28), privacy (4), telemetry payload shape (6), service exports + config (8), `fireAfterWriteEvaluator` hook (3 — 2xx fires, 5xx skips, null-id no-throw). Telemetry capture via monkey-patched `logger.info`. (11) **Cross-cutting cleanup — 6 prior test files patched** for canonical-only Company.stage enum (legacy stage seeds → canonical equivalents): 184a (`'in_progress' → 'active'`), 184d (`'in_progress' → 'active'`), 184e (3 lines), 23-190 wizard (2 lines), 23-191 (`'objective_identified' → 'active'`), 23-192b (2 lines), 24-246 migration (seeds via raw `Company.collection.insertOne` to bypass enum since migration's job is handling legacy). Resolved by dropping the redundant Move check (Move.weekly_goal_id is required, so any Move implies a parent WG; WG count check is sufficient). Strong: pre-coding scan immediately surfaced that backend was already shipped via #199 — saved an hour of redundant work; minimal-change-grounding held (additive backend, no rewrites); regression-first approach amended 5 prior tests proactively rather than reactively; vm-sandbox truth tables cleanly prove conditional rendering (Send-Assessment + ball-state) without needing browser; verify-then-delete on B-8 confirmed dead before deleting (sandbox simulation across 5 roles). One dead-end: initially tried to extract IIFE-internal functions for vm-sandbox via `module.exports`, then realized regex-extract-and-eval-in-context was simpler. **Architectural invariants verified**: ✅ Zero executable `switch-company` callers in `client/pages/scripts/` (#184c regression 32/32 green); ✅ `StageTransitionService` sole writer of `Company.stage` — new POST `/clients/:id/stage` calls `StageTransitionService.manualTransition` which wraps `companyStageInstance`; ✅ Lifecycle writer pattern preserved — no direct `Company.stage` writes added; ✅ "Ball state" remains UI render-mapping (client `renderBallStateLine` + server `bucketByLifecycle` helper); ✅ No new role-check sites outside phase3-3 (still 22 sites blessed; line 418→423 entry refreshed). **S25 prereq gate** (carried from #199): all 4 ✅ shipped; this session does not touch them. **Net session output**: 18 files changed (+1395/-438 lines: 2 new test files; 16 modified — 7 production source + 7 regression tests + 1 phase3-3 lint + 1 handoff + 1 SESSION_LOG.md). 1 commit `295cd0d` on `development`. **Next session**: `/coding` Session D re-ordered — Epic 24.2 (Profile tab page-reuse + dual-auth + email) + Epic 24.8 B-6 ride-along (Task.postpone_reason schema persistence). Risk R6 (`company-profile.html` audit before consultant write changes) becomes relevant. Estimated 5-6 pts (4-5 + 1), ~3 hours fresh session — substantial scope, fresh context recommended. |

Move schema had no field. The Task path in [planning-v2.js:931](client/pages/scripts/planning-v2.js#L931) `postponeTask(taskId, goalId)` has **no reason input modal at all** — it just moves the Task to next week's WG. Field placed on Move only; Task path stays unchanged (adding a Task reason input would be scope creep, no current UI captures one). (4) **`requireManagedClient` location** — spec hinted `server/middleware/auth.js`; actual is at [server/routes/consultant.js:666](server/routes/consultant.js#L666). PUT route uses `Object.assign(move, req.body)` — field flows through automatically; no allowlist change. (2) [client/pages/scripts/dashboard-v2.js:553-572](client/pages/scripts/dashboard-v2.js#L553-L572) `confirmPostpone()` — reads `#postpone-reason` textarea, sends `{due_date, postpone_reason}` in PUT body. (3) NEW [client/js/consultant-page-mode.js](client/js/consultant-page-mode.js) (~115 LOC) — `window.ConsultantPageMode` IIFE; pure functions; `detect()` reads URL params + `karvia_user` from localStorage and returns `{mode ∈ ['self','consultant','unauthorized'], clientId, fromWorkspace, isAuthorized}`; `renderBanner(companyName)` injects DOM only when consultant, idempotent re-renders (returns existing node, updates innerHTML), no-op + auto-cleanup on self mode; HTML escaped via inline `escapeHtml`; back-link target depends on `fromWorkspace` (workspace if true, else my-clients); CommonJS-export-compatible for Node test harness; constant `BANNER_ID = 'consultant-page-mode-banner'` exposed. (5) [client/js/company-profile.js](client/js/company-profile.js) — boot path branches on `ConsultantPageMode.detect()`; module-scoped `companyId = pageMode.mode === 'consultant' ? pageMode.clientId : user.company_id`; on `mode === 'unauthorized'` calls new `showUnauthorizedAndStop(pageMode)` which renders a minimal "Not authorized" panel with workspace/my-clients back-link; after `loadCompanyData() + populateForm()`, calls `ConsultantPageMode.renderBanner(companyData.name)`; `saveProfile()` untouched — already PUTs `/api/companies/${companyId}` so the override flows through; existing autosave + AI-Knows + maturity-badge init unchanged. Caught via `ls scripts/`, retried with correct names — both green. **Architectural invariants verified**: ✅ Multi-tenant isolation preserved — `PUT /api/companies/:id` uses existing `managed_businesses` membership; cross-tenant + non-managed both 403 in tests. ✅ Role-based access — server enforces auth via `requireRole` + `managed_businesses` in `companies.js` AND `requireManagedClient` on consultant routes; new frontend role check is **render-only** (BLESSED_SITES entry + source comment + named server-side chokepoints in why-note). ✅ XSS prevention — banner copy escapes `companyName` via inline `escapeHtml()`; back-link URL uses `encodeURIComponent(clientId)`. ✅ Soft-delete pattern unchanged. ✅ Move PUT remains sole writer for Move state; telemetry `move.postponed` (#192b) still fires on `due_date` change. ✅ No new `switch-company` callers; LLMGateway untouched. **S25 prereq gate** (carried from #199/#200): all 4 ✅ shipped; this session does not touch them. **Watch-items resolved**: R6 (`company-profile.html` audit before consultant write changes) — done, dual-trust verified end-to-end via in-memory mongo. **Quality reflection: 9/10**. | 202 | May 7 | Coding (Sprint 24 Session E re-ordered — Epic 24.9 shared TabHeader / KPI strip + Teams-tab KPIs) | ~1.5h | ~95K | 2 | 9/10 | Epic 24.9 SHIPPED — `client/css/components/tab-header.css` + `client/js/tab-header.js` (`KarviaTabHeader.renderKPIStrip()`) + Summary tab refactor (5 KPI cards + Last Activity tile) + Teams-tab KPI strip (4 cards) + dashboard-summary `objectives.lifecycle` extension + `/clients/:id/teams` `kpis` block + DESIGN_SYSTEM Component-CSS section; 73 net new assertions; 18/18 regression suites green ≈ 1075 assertions | **Phase A — pre-coding scan (Why-first per `feedback_why_what_how_when.md`)**: read Epic 24.9 spec + Summary tab `renderSummary` + Teams tab `renderTeams` + `dashboard-summary` payload + `/clients/:id/teams` payload + KarviaCommon API + DESIGN_SYSTEM + `bucketByLifecycle` helper + User.team_id schema + Assessment status enum. **Surfaced 4 spec ↔ reality items before code** (per `feedback_minimal_change_grounding.md` discipline): (1) **Summary card-count drift** — spec said "5 cards" but live Summary ships 6 (the 6th = `Last Activity`, a date+description tile). Resolution: 5 standard KPIs in shared strip + Last Activity as sibling tile below; helper exposes `valueHTML` field for raw-HTML escape hatch (Risk pill, future complex content). (2) **Objectives KPI bucket source drift** — spec wants ball-state distribution (`6 total · 🎯2 🤝3 📊1`, per Epic 24.1 D-Onion-4) but `dashboard-summary` returns only status buckets (`on_track / at_risk / behind`). Resolution: extend objectives query `.select('status updated_at lifecycle_stage')` and reuse the route-local `bucketByLifecycle` (same one #200 wired into `/portfolio-summary`). **Zero new round-trips** — F-M-02 5-cap intact. (3) **Teams KPI data gap** — `/clients/:id/teams` today returns only the team list; 3 of 4 spec KPI cards have no data path (ASSESSMENT COMPLETION, COVERAGE, AVG SSI BY TEAM). Resolution: extend same endpoint with `kpis` sibling block batched under existing `Promise.all` — 4 new aggregations (`Team.countDocuments`, `Assessment.aggregate` for status tally, `User.aggregate` with `$cond`/`$ifNull` for unassigned-employee + manager-without-team coverage, `Assessment.aggregate` with `$lookup` for per-team avg SSI). F-M-06 one-endpoint-per-tab intact. (4) **Sequencing inversion (resolved)** — spec line 114-115 said Epic 24.9 ships *before* 24.1+24.2 so those would consume the helper; per the post-#199 session re-order, Sessions C+D already shipped 24.1+24.2 with their own per-tab UI. Refactoring shipped tabs is OUT-OF-SCOPE (regression risk on #200/#201 wins). Helper now ships for Sessions F (24.3 P2) + G (24.4) to consume. **Phase B — locked plan with user via summary message**: 5+1 Summary, lifecycle bucket extension, 4-card Teams strip with extended endpoint payload, F-M-02/F-M-06 invariants preserved. **Phase C — implementation (7 todos)**: (1) NEW [client/css/components/tab-header.css](client/css/components/tab-header.css) (~85 LOC) — namespaced `.kts-*` (Karvia Tab Strip): `.kts-strip` (CSS grid `auto-fit minmax(180px,1fr)` 16px gap), `.kts-card` (4px left-border conveys semantic accent), accent variants `--on_track / --at_risk / --urgent / --neutral`, `.kts-label / .kts-value / .kts-trend / .kts-sublines`. Single mobile breakpoint at **720px** (D-Onion-2 acceptance) → cards stack to single column + value font shrinks 28→22px. Namespace doesn't collide with `.cw-summary-*` or `.cw-list` rules. (2) NEW [client/js/tab-header.js](client/js/tab-header.js) (~95 LOC) — `window.KarviaTabHeader` IIFE, CommonJS-export-compatible. Public API: `renderKPIStrip(cards, container)`. Card schema: `{ label, value, valueHTML?, trend?, accent?, sublines? }`. Idempotent re-renders (replaces innerHTML, no append). Empty cards → empty container (graceful no-op). XSS-safe: `label / value / sublines` HTML-escaped via inline `escapeHtml`; `valueHTML` documented as caller-trusted escape hatch. Bogus accent/trend silently dropped (whitelist via `Set` + 3-value enum). Trend glyphs `▲ / ▼ / →`. (3) MODIFY [client/pages/client-workspace.html](client/pages/client-workspace.html) — `<link rel="stylesheet" href="../css/components/tab-header.css">` (loaded BEFORE `client-workspace.css` per CSS cascade convention) + `<script src="/js/tab-header.js">` after `display-labels.js`, before `navigation.js`. (4) MODIFY [client/pages/scripts/client-workspace.js](client/pages/scripts/client-workspace.js): `renderTab` now passes `payload.kpis` to `renderTeams` (signature change `renderTeams(items, kpis)`); `renderSummary` refactored — builds 5-card array, Risk uses `valueHTML` for the existing pill markup, Objectives sublines = `🎯 ${lifecycle.identified} · 🤝 ${lifecycle.handed_off} · 📊 ${lifecycle.sustained}` per spec line 59 + Epic 24.1 D-Onion-4, computes semantic accents (Risk → on_track/at_risk/urgent mapping; Objectives → on_track when handed_off>0 OR sustained>0 else at_risk for non-zero totals; neutral for zero/N/A); `Last Activity` rendered as a separate `.cw-summary-card` tile below the strip — preserves the existing visual contract. NEW `renderTeamsKpiStrip(kpis)` helper composes 4 cards: TEAMS (count + members), ASSESSMENT COMPLETION (`X/Y` + `Z%`, semantic accent ≥80% green / 40-79% amber / <40% red), COVERAGE (urgent if mgrs-without-team>0; at_risk if unassigned>0; on_track otherwise), AVG SSI BY TEAM (top score on top, top-3 in subline). `renderTeams` now renders strip on top + list below; strip renders even when `items.length === 0` so consultants see coverage signals (e.g. `12 unassigned employees`) before any team is configured. (5) MODIFY [server/routes/consultant.js](server/routes/consultant.js): `dashboard-summary` objectives query `.select('status updated_at lifecycle_stage')`; response `objectives.lifecycle = bucketByLifecycle(objectives)` (no extra round-trip). `/clients/:id/teams` extended with 4 new aggregations under existing `Promise.all`; `kpis` block returned alongside `data` + `pageInfo`. `totalMembers` counts only team-assigned users (skips `_id: null` group) so the unassigned bucket isn't double-counted with COVERAGE card. Per-team avg SSI uses `$lookup` from Assessment to User then `$group` on `$user.team_id`. (6) MODIFY [.claude/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md](.claude/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md) — new "Component CSS" section (~65 LOC) documenting `client/css/components/` folder convention + `TabHeader / KPI strip` API spec + visual language + consumers list. (7) NEW [scripts/test-sprint24-249-tab-header.js](scripts/test-sprint24-249-tab-header.js) — **73 assertions** in 6 sections: [1/6] tab-header.js parse + DOM contract: empty cards → empty; single card with label/value/sublines/trend/accent rendered; 4 accent variants; 3 trend variants (up/down/flat); bogus accent + bogus trend silently dropped; XSS escape on label/value/sublines; `valueHTML` raw-HTML override; idempotent re-render replaces (not appends); null container no-throw. [2/6] tab-header.css selectors + 720px mobile breakpoint single-column rule. [3/6] client-workspace.html wires CSS+JS in correct order (tab-header.css before client-workspace.css). [4/6] client-workspace.js: Summary cards array + `KarviaTabHeader.renderKPIStrip` call + Last Activity preserved + lifecycle bucket sublines + Risk valueHTML; Teams `payload.kpis` passed through; `renderTeams(items, kpis)` signature; 4-card composition; strip renders on empty list. [5/6] In-memory mongo end-to-end: `dashboard-summary` returns `objectives.lifecycle` (`identified=1, handed_off=1, sustained=0`) alongside legacy buckets; `/clients/:id/teams` returns `kpis` with correct `teams_count=2 / total_members=3 / completed=2 / total=3 / completion_pct=67 / unassigned=1 / managers_without_team=1`; `avg_ssi_by_team` returns Sales=8.0 + Ops=6.0 sorted desc; non-managed → 403. [6/6] phase3-3 lint regression: `tab-header.js` has no role checks → no `BLESSED_SITES` entry needed. **Phase D — regression sweep — 18/18 suites green ≈ 1075 assertions**: phase3-3 (9, 23 sites), 22-cockpit ✓, 22-status-button ✓, 22-epic-c (36), 22-epic-c-phase3 (37), 22-epic-c-polish (40), 22a-184a (55), 22a-184b (52), 22a-184c (32), 22a-184d (36), 22a-184e (36), 23-190 (76), 23-191 (135), 23-192b ✓, 24-241 (73), 24-242 (43), 24-243 ✓, 24-246 ✓, 24-248 (36), 24-249 (73 NEW). **Phase E — bugs found and fixed mid-session** (3, all in test seed data — same recurring lesson as #201): (1) Team validation `manager_name` required — schema requires it even though `manager_id` is set; added `manager_name: 'Sage X'`. (2) User validation `password_hash` minlength 6 — used `'h'`, switched to `'hashed-pw'`. (3) Assessment status enum doesn't include `'pending'` — actual enum is `['draft','in_progress','completed','reviewed','archived']`; the dashboard/teams aggregations defensively sum both `pending` and `in_progress` _ids, so swapped test fixture to `'in_progress'`. **Same recurring fixture-fields lesson** from #198/#199/#200/#201 — should have read Team / User / Assessment validation rules upfront. (4) `totalMembers` semantic bug — initial impl summed all groups including `_id: null` (unassigned), inflated count from 3 → 5 (u1+u2 in Sales, u3 in Ops, but +u4 unassigned, +mgr no team). Fixed by filtering to `m._id ?` truthy in the reduce; added inline comment explaining the COVERAGE-card-doesn't-double-count rationale. **Phase F — handoff updates**: progress row #202 added (2 pts, 9/10); Total 17/29 (~59%); NEW "Epic 24.9 Outcome (Session #202)" subsection with 4 spec-drifts inventoried + implementation summary + invariants ✅ + run instructions; net-new findings #202 logged. **Architectural invariants verified**: ✅ Multi-tenant isolation preserved — `requireManagedClient` still gates `/clients/:id/*`; non-managed teams → 403 (in-memory test). ✅ F-M-02 dashboard-summary 5-cap intact — only added `lifecycle_stage` to existing query select; no 6th query (re-verified by 184a F-M-02 spy: still ≤7). ✅ F-M-06 one-endpoint-per-tab intact — Teams tab still calls only `/clients/:id/teams`; KPI block batched under existing `Promise.all`. ✅ XSS prevention — shared component HTML-escapes `label / value / sublines`; `valueHTML` documented as caller-responsibility (used only by Risk card with KarviaCommon-internal pill markup, no user input). ✅ No new role-check sites — `tab-header.js` is pure rendering; phase3-3 lint allow-list unchanged at 23 sites. ✅ Soft-delete pattern unchanged. ✅ Lifecycle writer chokepoint untouched — `bucketByLifecycle` is a read-side mapping helper, not a writer. **S25 prereq gate** (carried from #199/#200/#201): all 4 artifacts ✅ shipped; this session does not touch them. **Watch-items**: R10 (`team-ssi-view.html` audit before Session G) still open. **Quality reflection: 9/10**. Strong: pre-coding scan caught 4 spec ↔ reality items before any code (mirroring #198/#199/#200/#201 discipline) — including the Sequencing-inversion-resolved insight that prevents wasted refactor work on shipped tabs C+D; minimal-change held — `valueHTML` escape hatch instead of bespoke component variants for Risk pill; `Last Activity` preserved as sibling tile instead of forcing the KPI model to absorb non-KPI content; lifecycle bucket added with **zero round-trip cost** by extending existing `.select()`; F-M-02 + F-M-06 invariants verified; F-L-03 honored (no role checks added). Negative: **same recurring fixture-fields lesson** — 3 separate test seed validation errors (Team.manager_name, User.password_hash minlength, Assessment status enum) caught only at runtime; should have read full validation contract upfront. ~3 min wasted; **5th session in a row with this same lesson** — pattern is real, internalization isn't sticking. `totalMembers` semantic bug also caught at test runtime not at design — should have walked through the aggregation result shape with null `_id` group in mind. **Net session output**: 6 files changed (~+560/-55 lines: 3 new — `tab-header.css` (85 LOC), `tab-header.js` (95 LOC), `test-sprint24-249-tab-header.js` (310 LOC); 3 modified — `client-workspace.html` (+2 lines), `client-workspace.js` (~+90/-50), `consultant.js` (~+95/-5), `DESIGN_SYSTEM.md` (+65)). Git status clean post-test-run, ready for commit. **Next session**: `/coding` Session F — Epic 24.3 Part 2 (Objectives tab UI consuming the new `KarviaTabHeader.renderKPIStrip` for HEALTH/CONSTRAINT/DRIVERS/VELOCITY cards). Med scope (3-4 pts). Then Session G (24.4 Plan tab, also consumes the helper) and Session H (24.5 Assessments + sprint close). |
