| 204 | May 7 | Coding (Sprint 24 Session F-tail — Storage-key fragmentation cleanup, audit IDs A20260507-01/03/04) | ~1h | ~85K | 1 (audit-fix) | 9/10 | All 3 storage-key fragmentation clusters SHIPPED — auth-token cluster (`karvia_auth_token` ↔ `karvia_token`), user-blob cluster (`karvia_user` ↔ `user`), bare-`token` 3rd-tier removed; ~16 client files canonicalized; idempotent boot cleanup wired in BOTH `common.js` and `auth-check.js`; 23 new in `test-sprint24-204-storage-key-cleanup.js` + full S22+S22a+S23+S24+phase3 sweep 25/25 suites green; resolves user-reported BUG-S24-01 | **Trigger**: post-#203 dev verification on `karvia-business-1` showed My Clients page TOKEN_EXPIRED on `/portfolio-kpis` + `/portfolio-summary` even after re-login. **Diagnosis**: legacy `karvia_token` localStorage key shadowed canonical `karvia_auth_token` in [client/pages/scripts/my-clients.js:124-125](client/pages/scripts/my-clients.js#L124) (reverse read-order). Re-login replaced canonical key but never the stale legacy key, so jwt.verify saw a same-`JWT_SECRET` token with `exp` in the past. **Comprehensive sweep** (post-trigger, before fix) surfaced 3 parallel legacy-key clusters all rooted in the same anti-pattern (legacy + canonical keys both written, read-order varying by file): Cluster A (`karvia_auth_token` ↔ `karvia_token`), Cluster B (`karvia_user` ↔ `user`), Cluster C (bare un-prefixed `token` 3rd-tier fallback). Logged 4 stable audit IDs `A20260507-01/02/03/04` per governance (`A{YYYYMMDD}-{nn}`); user requested same-day fix instead of deferring to Session G. **Phase A — fix execution (~16 files touched)**: (1) [my-clients.js:124-125](client/pages/scripts/my-clients.js#L124) `getToken()` canonical-only (was the BUG-S24-01 root cause). (2) [common.js:664](client/js/common.js#L664) + [navigation.js:363](client/js/navigation.js#L363) — drop legacy `setItem('karvia_token', ...)` writes (the dual-write that perpetuated migration shadow forever). (3) Bulk-strip `\|\| getItem('karvia_token')` fallback chains across 12 files via perl one-liner `s/\s*\|\|\s*localStorage\.getItem\(['"]karvia_token['"]\)//g` + 2 manual fixes for multi-line variants ([planning-v2.js:264-265](client/pages/scripts/planning-v2.js#L264), [objectives.js:1559-1560](client/pages/scripts/objectives.js#L1559)). (4) [quarterly-review.js:27,478](client/pages/scripts/quarterly-review.js#L27) + [dashboard-v2.js:113](client/pages/scripts/dashboard-v2.js#L113) + [signup.html:659](client/pages/signup.html#L659) — convert `karvia_token`-only readers to canonical. (5) Cluster B fix: [dashboard.html:228](client/pages/dashboard.html#L228) (broken ternary `getItem('karvia_auth_token') ? getItem('user') : null` rewritten to read canonical `karvia_user`), [executive-dashboard.js:49](client/pages/scripts/executive-dashboard.js#L49) + [analytics-dashboard.js:22](client/pages/scripts/analytics-dashboard.js#L22) — `getItem('user')` → `getItem('karvia_user')`. (6) Cluster C fix: drop `\|\| getItem('token')` 3rd-tier from `auth-check.js`, `common.js` (×2), `my-clients.js`, `add-client-wizard.js`. (7) [auth-check.js:9-26](client/js/auth-check.js#L9) — extended migration shim: when canonical absent, promote legacy to canonical; then ALWAYS removeItem on all 3 legacy keys (idempotent across reloads). (8) [common.js:9-22](client/js/common.js#L9) — added parallel boot cleanup gated on `getItem('karvia_auth_token')` presence (covers pages that load common.js but not auth-check.js, e.g. `quarterly-goals.html`, `quarterly-review.html`). **Phase B — regression test** [scripts/test-sprint24-204-storage-key-cleanup.js](scripts/test-sprint24-204-storage-key-cleanup.js) (NEW, 23 assertions): static-grep `client/` tree asserts (1) only migration shim file may read legacy `karvia_token`; (2) only migration shim may read bare `token`; (3) zero files read bare `user`; (4) zero `setItem` for any legacy key (`karvia_token`, `token`, `user`); (5) boot cleanup wired in BOTH common.js + auth-check.js with the canonical-presence gate; (6) per-file canonical-only spot checks (my-clients.js getToken, dashboard-v2.js getToken, dashboard.html user-blob, etc.); (7) defense-in-depth — logout still removes all 5 keys. **Phase C — phase3-3 lint allow-list line-number sync**: my added boot-cleanup blocks shifted role-check sites — `auth-check.js` +8 lines (220→228), `common.js` +17 lines (640→657), `navigation.js` +1 line (423→424). Updated BLESSED_SITES line numbers; the role checks themselves are unchanged. **Phase D — regression sweep — 25/25 suites green** (phase3-1, phase3-3 (9), 22-cockpit, 22-status-button, 22-epic-c (36), 22-epic-c-phase3 (37), 22-epic-c-polish (40), 22a-184a (55), 22a-184b (52), 22a-184c (32), 22a-184d (36), 22a-184e (36), 23-188 (39), 23-189 (45), 23-190 (76), 23-191 (135), 23-192a (50), 23-192b (136), 24-204 (23 NEW), 24-241 (73), 24-242 (43), 24-243 (197), 24-246 (45), 24-248 (36), 24-249 (73)). **Phase E — audit governance**: master tracker [AUDIT_TRACKER.md](KARVIA_STRATEGY/3-DELIVERY/2-QA-AND-TESTING/AUDIT_TRACKER.md) section moved OPEN → FIXED — Storage-Key Fragmentation Audit; 3 code IDs advanced 📝→💻→✅; summary table OPEN -3 (12→9), FIXED +3 (42→45). Per-sprint handoff Audit History extended; Watch-Item BUG-S24-01 marked RESOLVED. Process ID `A20260507-02` remains as audit-checklist amendment for future `/audit` (synonymous-storage-key sweep rule). **Architectural invariants verified**: ✅ JWT_SECRET single source via `process.env` (no key swap); ✅ migration shim is the only legitimate legacy reader; ✅ logout still removes all 5 keys (defense in depth); ✅ no role-check semantics changed (phase3-3 still 23 sites blessed, just line-shifted). **Audit-lens gap captured** (`A20260507-02`): no prior audit swept for "synonymous storage keys with read-order mismatch across consumers" — this category of shadow-state bug survives logout/login cycles. Mitigation rule added: for every pair of synonymous storage keys, assert (1) one writer per key, (2) identical read-order across consumers, (3) no current code writes the legacy key. Same rule applies to query-param synonyms, header synonyms, DB-field synonyms during schema migrations. **Quality reflection: 9/10**. Strong: comprehensive sweep before code (caught 3 parallel clusters not just the 1 reported); minimal-change held at consumer level (canonical-only reads); migration shim retained intentionally (only legitimate legacy reader); idempotent boot cleanup at module load + belt-and-suspenders in 2 files. Negative: bulk perl one-liner missed multi-line fallback chains in 2 files (caught by re-grep, fixed manually); 3 BLESSED_SITES line-number drifts caught only at lint runtime. **Net session output**: 31 files (+345/-94: 1 new test, 28 client files canonicalized, 1 phase3-3 line-number sync, AUDIT_TRACKER + handoff). Commits `355d918` (initial sweep log) → `5fac1a6` (A20260507-01/02 audit IDs) → `c28eca3` (expand to A20260507-03/04 after broader sweep) → `a80e8bd` (code fix shipped). Sprint 24 progress: **21/29 pts (~72%)**. **Next session**: `/coding` Session G — Epic 24.4 Plan tab read-only tree. Watch-item R10 (`team-ssi-view.html` audit) becomes relevant. |
| 205 | May 8 | Coding (Sprint 24 Session G re-ordered — Epic 24.4 Plan tab read-only tree) | ~2h | ~110K | 4 | 9/10 | Epic 24.4 SHIPPED — Plan tab full 3-section render (KPI strip + Behaviors strip + Tree); backend `?include=tasks,moves` whitelist on quarterly+weekly endpoints; weekly endpoint UNION-extended for legacy `Goal{WEEKLY}+Tasks` when `include=tasks`; quarterly endpoint populates `objective_id` w/ embedded `key_results` so tree labels KR nodes; 75 new in `test-sprint24-244-plan-tab.js` + full sweep **26/26 suites green** ≈ 1405 assertions | **Phase A — pre-coding scan (Why-first)**: read Epic 24.4 spec + existing client-workspace.js renderPlan placeholder + `/api/weekly-goals/:keyResultId` UNION READ pattern (S23 #191) + KarviaTabHeader.renderKPIStrip wiring on Summary/Teams tabs + Move/Task/Goal model required-fields. **Surfaced 3 spec ↔ reality drifts before code**: (1) **`?include=tasks` semantics on consultant weekly endpoint** — spec L89-91 says "populates `tasks[]` for legacy `Goal{WEEKLY}`" but the consultant endpoint at `consultant.js:987` only queries new `WeeklyGoal` collection. Two interpretations: (a) accept-but-no-op, (b) UNION READ legacy half. Picked (b) to mirror `/api/weekly-goals/:keyResultId` from S23 #191 + spec L93 "When both: populates per source type" implies union semantics. Cost: ~50 LOC vs (a)'s 5 LOC; benefit: Plan tab tree actually shows legacy weeklies on real data. (2) **KR-title source for tree** — spec assumes KR titles available but the consultant `/objectives` endpoint at `consultant.js:786` strips `key_results[]` from its projection (only returns `kr_rollup` totals). Quarterly Goal's `key_result_id` has no Mongoose `ref` field so `.populate('key_result_id')` won't auto-resolve. Solved by adding `.populate('objective_id', 'title key_results')` on the quarterly endpoint so the parent Objective + its embedded KR subdoc both ride along — single $in lookup per page slice; frontend resolves KR title via `objective_id.key_results.id(key_result_id)`. (3) **`.status-pill` is stale spec** — spec L81 said reuse `.status-pill` styles "from S22a #184b"; actual class in `client-workspace.css:244` is `.cw-status-chip`. Used existing `.cw-status-chip` class instead of creating an alias — one less name to maintain. **Phase B — locked plan with user**: 8-step sequence (backend → KPI strip → Behaviors → Tree → CSS → tests → handoff); sparkline as styled flex divs (not SVG — 30 LOC lighter, matches existing Karvia idiom; no SVG anywhere else in client-workspace.css); cache TTL = match existing tabs (no in-session expiry; refetch on tab re-entry only first time). User green-lit. **Phase C — implementation (8 todos, in order)**: (1) [server/routes/consultant.js](server/routes/consultant.js) — NEW `parseInclude(raw)` helper near pagination utilities; whitelist `Set(['tasks','moves'])`; returns `{ok:true, set}` or `{ok:false, bad:'<token>'}`; empty/missing → ok+empty-set. Both quarterly+weekly endpoints validate at top; reject invalid token with 400 + error message naming the bad token. Quarterly adds `.populate('objective_id', 'title key_results')` (additive — fires on every fetch including non-Plan-tab consumers, but payload bump is small). Weekly endpoint extended: when `include=moves`, single `Move.find({weekly_goal_id: {$in: wgIds}}).lean()` + bucket by `weekly_goal_id` and attach as `g.moves`; when `include=tasks`, separate `Goal.find({time_period:'WEEKLY', ...same filter axes})` + `Task.find({goal_id: {$in}}).lean()` + bucket by `goal_id` + tag legacy items `_source='legacy'` + new items `_source='new'`; appended after the new-source page slice; pagination cursor still walks new collection only (legacy is read-only continuity until S25 Epic 25.1 cutover); response gains `sources: {new, legacy}` block when include=tasks. (2-5) [client/pages/scripts/client-workspace.js](client/pages/scripts/client-workspace.js) — `loadAndRenderTab` special-cases `tab === 'plan'` to call NEW `loadPlanData()` which `Promise.all` fetches `dashboard-summary`+`objectives`+`goals/quarterly?limit=200`+`goals/weekly?include=tasks,moves&limit=200`, reusing `state.cache.summary`/`state.cache.objectives` if sibling tabs already populated them (saves 0-2 reqs on first activation). NEW renderPlan replaces ~45-LOC quarterly/weekly toggle placeholder with 3-section render. NEW `renderPlanKpiStrip()` builds 4 cards via `KarviaTabHeader.renderKPIStrip()` — card 1 (THIS WEEK) counts WGs in current ISO week + KRs uncovered (KRs with no QG); card 2 (TODAY) counts moves due today across all WGs + done; card 3 (CATCH-UP) counts pushed (`postpone_reason` set) + forgotten (past due_date, not completed) for both Moves + Tasks; card 4 (FRESHNESS) max(updatedAt) across objectives+WGs as relative-date string + `Plan to:` last planned-week. Accent semantics: catch-up urgent if forgotten>0; today on_track if doneToday>=dueToday; this-week at_risk if uncoveredKRs>0. NEW `groupBehaviors(moves)` — groups recurring Moves (`frequency != 'once'`) by lowercased+trimmed `(title, discipline)` tuple; computes 6-cell sparkline by ISO-week-relative-to-current (range -5..0); completion ratio across all completions[]; last_completed_at via max(date where done=true) ∪ move.completed_at; sort by completion_ratio DESC then last_completed_at DESC. NEW `renderSparkline(weeks)` — 6 styled `<span class="cw-spark-cell">` divs with `is-filled` modifier per cell. NEW `renderBehaviorsStrip(recurringMoves)` — section header "BEHAVIORS recurring; last 6 weeks · v1 (title-grouped)"; empty state "No recurring behaviors yet for this client". NEW `renderPlanTree({objectives, quarterly, weekly})` — builds KR catalog via populated `objective_id.key_results`, attaches QGs by `key_result_id`, attaches WGs to QGs by `parent_goal_id`, KR-standalone WGs grouped under virtual "Weekly plans (no quarterly parent)" node, orphan QGs (no KR match) grouped under virtual "Quarterly goals without a KR" node, top-level driver-aware empty state when nothing exists. NEW `renderKRNode/QGNode/WGNode/LeafNode` recursive renderers — KR/QG/WG levels color-coded via left-border (indigo→violet→purple); current-ISO-week WG gets emerald background + "This week" tag + `data-expanded="true"`; Tasks `📋` and one-off Moves `🎯` as leaves with completion icons (✓/⏸/○); driver-aware empty states embedded at every empty level ("No quarterly goals yet — Manager creates the plan." / "No weekly plan yet — Manager builds week-by-week." / "No tasks/moves logged this week."). NEW `wirePlanInteractions(panel)` — single `addEventListener('click')` per `.cw-tree-toggle` toggling `aria-expanded` + sibling `.cw-tree-children[hidden]`; pure read-only — zero PUT/POST/DELETE/PATCH calls anywhere in renderPlan. NEW ISO helpers `isoWeekStart`, `isoWeekNumber`, `currentISOWeek` inlined (planning-v2.js's helpers are IIFE-private). Removed: old quarterly/weekly toggle (`renderPlan(items)` placeholder + `state.planView` sub-state). (6) [client/css/client-workspace.css](client/css/client-workspace.css) — additive 175-line section: `.cw-plan-strip` margin; `.cw-section-heading` shared title+meta layout; `.cw-panel-empty--inline` for inline empty states; `.cw-behaviors-strip` + `.cw-behavior-row` (24-1fr-auto grid w/ emoji+title-meta+sparkline) + `.cw-behavior-meta` (chip + ratio + last-done); `.cw-chip-discipline` (indigo bg, indigo-text); `.cw-spark-cell` (8×14px gray) + `.cw-spark-cell.is-filled` (emerald); `.cw-tree-root` + nested `.cw-tree-children[hidden]` + 12px-left-padded `.cw-tree-children` w/ left border; `.cw-tree-toggle` (full-width button, hover bg, focus-visible blue outline) + `.cw-tree-caret` (CSS-triangle rotates on `[data-expanded="true"]`); `.cw-tree-label`/`.cw-tree-sub`/`.cw-tree-count`/`.cw-tree-tag--current`; level-coded `.cw-tree-node-kr/qg/wg > .cw-tree-toggle` left-border colors (indigo `#3730A3`, violet `#6366F1`, purple `#8B5CF6`); `.cw-tree-node-wg.is-current-week > .cw-tree-toggle` emerald accents; `.cw-tree-node--virtual > .cw-tree-toggle` dashed border; `.cw-tree-empty` italic muted; `.cw-tree-leaf` 4-col grid (icon, status, title, type) w/ hover bg. (8) AMEND [scripts/test-sprint22a-184b-client-workspace.js](scripts/test-sprint22a-184b-client-workspace.js) — old `'Plan tab toggle reloads via single endpoint per gesture'` assertion encoded the retired quarterly/weekly toggle (`/loadedTabs\.delete\(['"]plan['"]\)/`); replaced with 2 assertions matching the new contract: (a) `loadAndRenderTab` special-cases `tab === 'plan'` and calls `loadPlanData()`; (b) `loadPlanData` reuses summary cache via `Promise.resolve(state.cache.summary)`. **Phase D — regression sweep**: 26 suites — phase3-1, phase3-3 (9 — 23 sites blessed), 22-cockpit, 22-status-button, 22-epic-c (36), 22-epic-c-phase3 (37), 22-epic-c-polish (40), 22a-184a (55), 22a-184b (53 — was 52, +1 net), 22a-184c (32), 22a-184d (36), 22a-184e (36), 23-188 (39), 23-189 (45), 23-190 (76), 23-191 (135), 23-192a (50), 23-192b (136), 24-204 (23), 24-241 (73), 24-242 (43), 24-243 (197), 24-244 (**75 NEW**), 24-246 (45), 24-248 (36), 24-249 (73). **26/26 suites green ≈ 1405 assertions.** **Phase E — bugs found and fixed mid-session** (3): (1) Test fixture validation cascade — same recurring lesson from #198/#199/#200/#201: should grep `required|enum` on all touched models BEFORE writing `Model.create({...})`. Goal model requires `due_date` AND `week` even on QUARTERLY rows (legacy schema cap pre-#191 era); Task model requires `objective_id` AND uses `name` not `title` AND requires `due_date`; Move's `discipline` is enum-restricted to disciplines from `config/disciplines.js` (`'sales'` is invalid; canonical IDs are `truth/ownership/follow_through/foresight/alignment`). 3 separate validation errors caught during first test run; fixed in 2 minutes via `node -e "console.log(require('./server/config/disciplines').getIds())"` + reading model files. **Lesson: this is now session #6 with the same fixture-fields cost — adding to memory `feedback_test_fixture_validation` is paying back; should also grep schema BEFORE first write attempt next time.** (2) Side-effect of `Edit replace_all` — bulk-replaced `'sales'` → `'truth'` for the Move seeds (model enum); but the same string appeared in a JS-side-only fixture (case-insensitive grouping test in vm-sandbox), where the same change broke the case-collapse test (`'truth'` vs `'Sales'` are now genuinely different). Caught on first test run (1/75 failure: "Case-insensitive trim grouping → 1 group — got 2"). Fixed by changing the JS-side fixture to `'Truth'` vs `'truth'` (intra-test case-insensitive comparison). **Lesson: `replace_all` is dangerous when the string straddles a real-vs-mock boundary.** (3) Test-grader regex too strict — initial sweep grader looked for `═══ N/N assertions passed ═══` exact pattern, but suites use varied tail patterns ("All 76/76 assertions green", "ALL 36 ASSERTIONS PASSED", "Sprint 24 #204: 23 assertions, 0 failed", "═══ 39/39 green — Sprint 23 #188 ═══"). Initial grader reported 1/26 passing when actually 26/26 were green. Loosened to OR-of-known-patterns + negative check on `✗ N failure` on second run. **Architectural invariants verified**: ✅ Multi-tenant isolation — both quarterly + weekly endpoints filter by `req.managedCompanyId` via `consultantClientGuards`; cross-tenant test 403 via requireManagedClient. ✅ RBAC enforcement — `requireRole('CONSULTANT')` still gates both endpoints (test seeds non-managed company2). ✅ XSS prevention — every dynamic value in renderPlan/renderBehaviorsStrip/renderTree goes through `esc()` (KarviaCommon.escapeHtml); spec L81 status pill class corrected `.status-pill` → `.cw-status-chip`. ✅ Soft delete — both endpoints filter `status: { $ne: 'cancelled' }`; Move query in include=moves does the same. ✅ Read-only — renderPlan + wirePlanInteractions have ZERO HTTP write methods (asserted in test). ✅ No new role-check sites outside phase3-3 (still 23 blessed). **S25 prereq gate** (carried from #199/#200/#201/#202): all 4 ✅ shipped; this session does not touch them. **Watch-items**: R10 (team-ssi-view.html audit before write changes) → not relevant for this session per spec ("This epic does NOT touch write paths"); Plan tab targets client-workspace.html/.js, not team-ssi-view.html. **Quality reflection: 9/10**. Strong: pre-coding Why → What → How rubric caught 3 spec drifts before code; minimal-change held — UNION extension on weekly endpoint mirrored S23 #191's existing pattern instead of inventing new; sparkline as styled divs not SVG (~30 LOC saved); inline-helpers for ISO-week math instead of refactoring planning-v2.js IIFE; proactive test-184b amendment (the retired-toggle assertion would have failed silently as a stale-spec test). Negative: same recurring fixture-fields lesson — 6th session in a row paying ~3 min cost; should hard-internalize "grep `required|enum` on every touched model before first `.create()` call"; replace_all blast-radius lesson is new. **Net session output**: 6 files changed (+1190/-44 lines: 1 new — `test-sprint24-244-plan-tab.js` (442 LOC); 5 modified — `server/routes/consultant.js` (+106), `client/pages/scripts/client-workspace.js` (+388/-44), `client/css/client-workspace.css` (+175), `scripts/test-sprint22a-184b-client-workspace.js` (+12/-3), `KARVIA_STRATEGY/.../SPRINT24_HANDOFF_DOCUMENT.md`). Sprint 24 progress: **25/29 pts (~86%)**. **Next session**: `/coding` Session H re-ordered — Epic 24.5 (Assessments tab + Sprint close). 3-4 pts. Final Sprint 24 work session before sprint-review. |

BO created first objective (3 KRs) + first quarterly goal + first weekly goal; Manager assigned + completed first task. **Phase C — gap catalog (30 findings, severity-graded)**: 24 already covered by S25/26/27 plans (cross-env link bleed → EMAIL_DEEP_LINK_CONTRACT G2b; embedded vs standalone KR _id divergence → PX-3.6/3.18; two goal collections w/ disjoint schemas → PX-3.7/3.8/3.14/3.15/3.16; transition-emit emails unbuilt → S26 Workstream B; KR-aggregation cascade → S27 spike); **6 surfaced new and not in plan** → minted `A20260508-01..06`. **Phase D — `/strategy` amendment** (combined session): 6 IDs added to AUDIT_TRACKER + S25 master plan + S25 handoff. Bucketing: 4 to Phase 2 unconditional (PX-2.6 MECE seeder is_active fix CRITICAL · PX-2.7 retire pre-MECE JSON HIGH · PX-2.8 onboarding state machine wiring HIGH · PX-2.9 assessment_templates orphan cleanup MEDIUM); 2 to Phase 3 (PX-3.19 goals route field-name unify MEDIUM · PX-3.20 Goal.week conditional required HIGH). **Confirmed-via-journey**: PX-2.6/2.7/2.8/2.9 are unconditional (no Phase 1 dependency) since the journey already verified the leaks empirically — saves ~2 days of Phase 1 verification on these specific items. **Hard journey blocker discovered**: `seedMECEQuestions.js` defaults `is_active:false`; `/api/assessments/start` filters on `is_active:true` → 0/50 questions match → assessment cannot start out-of-box. Folded into existing PX-4.x service consolidation rather than a new ID since it's already in S25 scope. **Cascade verification — works AT goal-level, breaks ABOVE**: task-complete → goal: ✅ (status='completed', current_value 70→80, metrics.completed_tasks=1, completion_rate=100). Each prompt was self-contained, cited file:line for every claim, and produced a "NOT FOUND" call-out with the exact grep when something was absent. **Phase B — gate report**: 🟢 PX-1.6 GREEN — all 4 S24 prereqs verified shipped: (1) `Company.stage` 6-stage canonical at [`server/constants/companyStages.js:12-19`](../server/constants/companyStages.js#L12-L19) imported by [`Company.js:89-94`](../server/models/Company.js#L89-L94); (2) `Objective.lifecycle_stage` 6-stage canonical at [`server/constants/objectiveLifecycle.js:11-18`](../server/constants/objectiveLifecycle.js#L11-L18); (3) `LifecycleTransitionService.js` factory + 2 instances (companyStage + objectiveLifecycle) with idempotent `findOneAndUpdate` `fromStage` filter (lines 88-122) — sole writer; (4) `display-labels.js` populated with `COMPANY_STAGE_LABEL` (71-78), `OBJECTIVE_LIFECYCLE_LABEL` (56-63), `CONSULTANT_BALL_VIEW` (93-100), helpers `companyStageView()` / `lifecycleView()` (105-140). Git evidence: `6456832` (24.6) / `90b0a25` (24.3 P1) / `106d095` (24.3 P2) / `d34d753` (24.3 close). **Phase C — Phase 2 conditional decisions** (per Phase 1 outcomes): PX-2.1 DEFER (SSI narrative is lazy-on-read, acceptable for current load — refinement track); PX-2.2 FIRE (no inline `notifyTransition()` helper today — closest is per-route `fireAfterWriteEvaluator()` at [`LifecycleTransitionService.js:423-437`](../server/services/LifecycleTransitionService.js#L423-L437); per cross-sprint audit Group 2a, must be inline NOT event-bus); PX-2.3 NOT NEEDED (`showEmptyState()` already defined at [`objectives.js:662`](../client/pages/scripts/objectives.js#L662) — confirms #206b spike); PX-2.4 NOT NEEDED (full `ai-okr-review.html` page + 4 endpoints exist); PX-2.5 FIRE (BE endpoint exists at [`objective-wizard.js:488`](../server/routes/objective-wizard.js#L488), FE wired in wizard only — small standalone wire-up). **Phase D — Phase 4 conditional decisions**: PX-4.1 DEFER (`AIObjectivePlanner` single-purpose with 1 caller — consolidatable but low value); PX-4.2 KEEP (`/api/ai-okr/*` and `/api/objective-wizard/*` are NOT redundant — distinct UX flows; formally retained); PX-4.3 FIRE (`OKRRecommendationService` is dead code — only `testSSIDiagnostic.js:38` references it). **Phase E — 🚨 critical PX-1.11 finding**: 8 files still read embedded `Objective.key_results[]` — 7 NOT-MIGRATED (objectives.js / ai-okr.js / objective-wizard.js / consultant.js / cascade.js / planning.js / moves.js) + 1 HYBRID (weekly-goals.js prefers standalone but falls back to embedded) + 2 already MIGRATED (key-results.js / LifecycleTransitionService.js). PX-3.6 (drop embedded write at POST sites) and PX-3.18 (drop schema field at [`Objective.js:383`](../server/models/Objective.js#L383)) are NOT SAFE today. Proposed 8 new sub-tasks PX-3.6a–PX-3.6h (one per consumer file); these MUST ship before PX-3.6/3.18 are safe. Folded as "proposed sub-tasks" in handoff progress table — sprint scope shifts from 47→55 if all 8 surface as separate IDs vs folded into a single PX-3.6 macro (decision deferred to next session). **Phase F — refinement-track items captured** (NOT in S25 scope): PX-1.2 → eager narrative if dashboard load profile changes; PX-1.8 → 12-block DiagnosticReport fetched into context but not exposed to refine-objective LLM prompt at [`single-objective.js:181-226`](../server/prompts/endpoint-templates/single-objective.js#L181-L226) (only dimension-level weak/strong areas at lines 184-185); PX-1.10 → `aiOKRService` + `AIObjectivePlanner` consolidation candidate; KR_AGG G1 → S27 KR-aggregation cron design (no scheduler today, formula = average KR `progress_percentage` at read time per [`Objective.js:491-510`](../server/models/Objective.js#L491-L510), KR formula `(current - baseline) / (target - baseline) * 100` per [`KeyResult.js:104-109`](../server/models/KeyResult.js#L104-L109)). **Phase G — handoff updates**: progress table flipped Phase 1 to ✅ SEALED, "Done" 3→14 (11 verification + 3 from Day 1); added Phase 1 Outcomes section with full conditional decision tables; added Day 2 sign-off paragraph; genome `2026-05-08`→`2026-05-09`; total task count framing changed from "47" to "42 confirmed + 8 proposed" pending sub-task decision. **Quality reflection: 9/10**. Strong: parallel agent fan-out kept main-thread context lean (3 clusters in 1 round vs 11 sequential file-reads); citation discipline enforced via prompt template (every finding has `file.js:LINE` link); gate-first reporting pattern surfaced PX-1.6 GREEN before any other reasoning could proceed; refinement-track items captured separately so they don't pollute S25 scope; PX-1.11 finding (8 not-migrated consumers) raised as a scope question to the user rather than silently absorbed. Negative: subagents don't have file-write authority for audit artifacts — Cluster A reported PX-1.7 inline rather than creating `KR_AGGREGATION_AND_LIFECYCLE_AUDIT.md`; created it in main thread (~5 min recovery, not blocking but adds friction). Memory candidate: in future Phase-1-style discovery sessions, give one cluster explicit write authority for its deliverable doc with a clear path. **Phase H — extended-day code shipped (after user said "continue")**: User locked **PX-3.6a-h as 8 separate IDs** (master plan 47→55 task count, `routes/objectives.js` / `ai-okr.js` / `objective-wizard.js` / `consultant.js` / `cascade.js` / `planning.js` / `moves.js` + HYBRID `weekly-goals.js` each become own ID; PX-3.6 + PX-3.18 prereqs amended to require ALL of a-h ✓). **PX-4.3 ship**: `grep` confirmed only `testSSIDiagnostic.js:38` referenced `OKRRecommendationService` (verified `DiagnosticEngine.js:905` has its OWN local `generateOKRRecommendations` with different signature — unrelated). Deleted `server/services/diagnostic/OKRRecommendationService.js` (438 LOC); cleaned `server/scripts/testSSIDiagnostic.js` (import + Test 5 section + summary line). **PX-2.5 demotion (mid-batch finding)**: started reading `/api/objective-wizard/regenerate-kr` to mirror its pattern; discovered it's wizard-session-bound (validates `WizardSessionService.getSession()`, writes via `WizardSessionService.updateKR()`, never touches persisted `Objective`/`KeyResult` collections). Cannot be called for saved Objectives whose wizard session has expired. PX-2.5 was scoped as "small FE wire-up" — reality requires a NEW endpoint (`POST /api/objectives/:id/key-results/:krId/regenerate` + service + FE button + tests ≈ 2-3h). Surfaced to user with options (defer to own session vs cut from sprint vs build now); user picked **defer**. Patched [SPRINT_X_VERIFICATION.md](../KARVIA_STRATEGY/2-TECHNICAL/SPRINT_X_VERIFICATION.md) PX-1.4 finding to reflect endpoint reality instead of the optimistic synthesis from earlier in the session. **PX-2.2 ship**: per cross-sprint audit Group 2a, added module-scoped `async function notifyTransition({service, docId, fromStage, toStage, actor, triggeredById, triggeredByKind, hasNote})` at top of [`server/services/LifecycleTransitionService.js`](../server/services/LifecycleTransitionService.js#L33-L72) — no-op stub today; S26 lands inline `if (service === ... && toStage === ...)` branches per Group 2a (NO event-bus, NO listener-registry). Call site inside `_record()` flip path immediately after telemetry emit (lines 130-148): `module.exports.notifyTransition({...}).catch(err => console.error(...))` — fire-and-forget, errors swallowed + logged, never propagated. Exported from module for spy-testability. **Test [`scripts/test-sprint25-209-notify-transition.js`](../scripts/test-sprint25-209-notify-transition.js) 36/36 ✓** in 8 sections: helper exported + async; payload shape (8 fields exact, alphabetized key check); fires on flip path for both `companyStage` + `objectiveLifecycle` instances; NOT fire on idempotent no-op (fromStage filter mismatch); NOT fire on history-only entry (assessment_completed marker); manualTransition catalog flips fire; thrown errors caught + logged + flip success unaffected; default ship is no-op (returns undefined). **Fixture validator self-correction (8th consecutive session paying recurring fixture-cost)**: initial Objective fixture missed `target_year` + `created_by` required fields → mongoose validation error → 5 sec recovery (grepped `required.*true` on Objective.js). Per memory `feedback_test_fixture_validation`: this is the EIGHTH session paying fixture cost; pattern is durable; should escalate from memory note to a pre-test scripted check (e.g., `node scripts/lint-test-fixture.js <model>` that pre-validates required fields). **Regression sweep — 342 assertions green**: S24 #243 197/197 ✓ + S22a #184e 36/36 ✓ + S24 #241 73/73 ✓ + new #209 36/36 ✓. **Audit tracker**: `A20260506-02` (G2a `notifyTransition` foundation) flipped 📝 → 💻 ✓ + last-updated `2026-05-09`; ✅ deferred to sprint close per workflow convention. **Quality reflection: 9/10**. Strong: parallel agent fan-out for Phase 1 (3 clusters in 1 round vs 11 sequential reads); citation discipline enforced via prompt template; gate-first reporting pattern surfaced PX-1.6 GREEN before reasoning could proceed; PX-2.5 reality-check surfaced mid-batch (refused to silently expand scope from "small wire-up" to full feature) — surfaced to user with options instead of unilateral expansion; PX-2.2 design honors Group 2a (inline branches, NO event-bus) and exposes via module-export-replace pattern for testability rather than a more invasive DI; all regressions green pre-commit. Negative: original Phase 1 synthesis for PX-1.4 was optimistic ("BE endpoint exists; FE wire-up only" without reading the endpoint body); patched mid-session but the optimistic framing made it into the verification doc first. Lesson: when a Cluster B agent reports "BE endpoint exists", verify by reading the endpoint body for session/state-binding before scoping a Phase 2 task. Memory candidate: add "endpoint scope check" rule to feedback memory — confirm endpoint is callable in target context (saved doc vs in-flight session) before scoping wire-up tasks. **Commit `b447b9c`**: 10 files (715 insertions / 577 deletions; net -3 files: -1 retired service + -1 cleaned test script section + +2 new artifacts). **Next session**: `/coding` Day 3 — recommend **PX-2.8** (onboarding state machine wiring) FIRST since PX-2.2's helper is now in place to receive S26 subscribers AND PX-2.8 explicitly couples with it (each onboarding tick = transition entry-point per `A20260508-05`). Then PX-2.7 (legacy seeder retire — strategy-decided Day 1) + PX-2.9 (orphan template archive — strategy-decided Day 1). Then a **PX-3.6a/g burst** (start with smaller routes `moves.js` + `cascade.js`) to validate the migration pattern before tackling the larger ones (`objectives.js` + `ai-okr.js`). PX-2.5 deferred to its own dedicated session post-PX-2.8. |

Per `Why → What → How → When` — the Why ("first MIGRATED canary surfaces data-readiness before PX-3.6 macro spreads the fallback drop to 7 more routes") drove the choice to drop the fallback completely rather than merely tagging existing HYBRID code with a lineage comment (the PX-3.6c-style formality wouldn't fit — the spec here is genuinely a behavior change). Strong: surgical scope kept diff to ~30 lines + 1 import drop + 1 new test file; 5 negative-shape assertions in section [1] exhaustively pin the deletion against re-introduction (you can't accidentally re-add `'key_results._id'`, the `Objective` import, or the `objWithEmbedded`/`krFound` identifiers without breaking the test); preserved Sprint 23 #191 union-read contract via 4 explicit positive assertions in section [4] so future migrations can't silently regress the legacy-data path; lineage chain (Sprint 25 PX-3.6h references PX-1.11 + PX-3.6 macro + PX-3.7 backfill + PX-3.18 schema drop) makes next reader's audit trail complete; first-run test pass on regex (no iteration) because schema/file shape was greppped upfront — broken the iteration streak again; explicit `process.exit(0)` applied prophylactically based on #215's lesson, no hang on first sweep run. Negative: didn't think to verify the FE caller path (`planning-v2.js`) until reflection — should have been part of the Why-first sweep before code change; per memory `Two-app model`, both Consultant and Client apps may hit this endpoint and a 404 in one app could surface differently than the other. Mitigation: 404 message body identical to pre-#216 ("Key result not found") so error-display surfaces in both apps stay byte-identical; no FE change needed. **Next session**: `/coding` Day 10 — recommend **PX-3.6 macro** (drop embedded write at all POST routes simultaneously: `objectives.js`, `ai-okr.js` ×5 sites, `objective-wizard.js` finalize) AND drop the 7 HYBRID embedded-read fallbacks (`consultant.js`, `cascade.js`, `planning.js` ×3 sites, `moves.js`, plus `objectives.js` read-side if any). After PX-3.6 ✓, PX-3.7 (backfill `--dry-run` + `_migrated_to`) + PX-3.8 (legacy POST/PUT/DELETE → 410 Gone) + PX-3.9-12 (pre-prod dry-run, regression, rollback doc, test-data execution) + PX-3.18 (drop `Objective.key_results[]` schema field entirely). Phase 5 sprint-close (PX-5.1–5.4) still pending — final 4 firing tasks before sprint close. |

Recommended (B) starting with objectives.js. **User decision**: "lets not delete anything for now, for safety reasons, lets close and start a new session if required — no compromise of safety". Validated stance: master-plan task line saying "drop X" is a *direction*, not a *license*; safety net must land first. **Memory captured**: NEW `feedback_no_destructive_without_greenlight.md` — encodes the rule that any task whose verb is "drop/delete/remove/retire/block" must surface a 2-3 sentence scope sketch + tradeoff to the user *before* the first edit, recommend a slice if diff exceeds ~3 files OR touches FE response shapes OR has no backfill path, and halt fully if user signals stop or safety. Also documents the canonical S25 sequence (PX-3.6 → PX-3.7 backfill → PX-3.8 410-Gone → PX-3.18 schema drop) as the model: removal is the LAST step, never the first. **Memory rules honored**: per `Stay grounded — minimal change` — surfaced scope BEFORE any edit; per `Why → What → How → When` — user's "no compromise of safety" answered the Why ahead of any What. Per `Two-app model` — implicitly noted that FE breakage from the embedded-array drop would land in BOTH Consultant + Client app surfaces (planning-v2.js + ai-okr-review.js are shared/parallel rendering paths). **Audit IDs**: none flipped (no code change). **Quality reflection: 10/10**. Pulled SPRINT26_PAGE_MATRIX E.1/E.2 specs + read [server/services/OnboardingProgressService.js:1-120](../server/services/OnboardingProgressService.js#L1-L120) + grep'd `ssi_result` / `ssi_scores` / `assessment_scores` across Assessment.js + Company.js + read [Assessment.js:300-340](../server/models/Assessment.js#L300-L340) + [Assessment.js:480-580](../server/models/Assessment.js#L480-L580) schemas. **Key grounding finding (worth preserving in S26-E.2 working notes)**: Assessment model actually **dual-writes** `ssi_scores` (legacy, 0-100 scale, set via [Assessment.js:844-847](../server/models/Assessment.js#L844-L847) with `// Also update legacy ssi_scores for backward compatibility` comment) AND `ssi_result.dimensions.{speed,strength,intelligence}.score` (canonical, 0-10 scale, set via UnifiedSSIScoringService). The drift risk isn't "one field missing" — it's **scale inversion**: `ssi_scores` is 0-100, `ssi_result.dimensions.*.score` is 0-10. The E.2 fix needs to **either** rescale on read OR have OnboardingProgressService consume the 0-10 dimensions and rescale to the 0-100 surface that `Company.assessment_scores.{speed,strength,intelligence,overall}_score` expects (Company schema at [Company.js:317](../server/models/Company.js#L317)). [`/team-breakdown` at assessments.js:758-761](../server/routes/assessments.js#L758-L761) iterates `Team.find({ company_id, is_active })` and counts members-with-completed-assessment PER TEAM ROW. Companies with 3 Assessment docs but 0 Team rows (BO-only shop / 5-person shop — same scenario as the S26 E.1 BO-only-shop carve-out, exactly the gap E.1 fixed for SSI tiles but didn't reach the KPI denominator) get `total_completed=0, total_members=0`. Canonical data lives at [`/company-results` at assessments.js:2236-2243](../server/routes/assessments.js#L2236-L2243) — direct `Assessment.find({ company_id, status: { $ne: 'excluded' } })` + dedupe-per-user. **Fix sketch** (~12-15 LoC BE, /team-breakdown response builder): bypass Team-row gating for headline count — `total_completed = Assessment.distinct('user_id', { company_id, status:'completed', user_id:{ $ne: null } }).length`; `total_members = User.countDocuments({ company_id, is_active: true, role: { $ne: 'CONSULTANT' } })`. Split rather than fold because: (1) different endpoint, (2) different fix surface, (3) BO/EXEC want company-wide semantics while MANAGER personal scope is correct as-is. **Fix sketch** (~6-8 LoC BE, role-conditional filter): drop `sent_by` filter for BUSINESS_OWNER + EXECUTIVE; keep for MANAGER. (2) BO/Manager team-ssi-view.html KPI header shows "0/0 Completed" / "0 Total Responses" while same client's assessment-hub Team Results tab renders 3 real respondents. **Two over-engineered framings rejected by user pushback in sequence — the core narrative of this session**: ROUND 1 — initial brainstorm proposed three "How" options (H1 derived cohort from `template_id + sent_at`; H2 explicit `cohort_id` field; H3 full `AssessmentCohort` collection) with my recommendation = H1+park H2; user reframed hard ("assessment template can be anything; SSI is one of many; AI Readiness, role-based, function, value, culture all valid; same template sent N times to same company"), correctly diagnosed I was treating SSI as **the** assessment and templates as variants instead of treating Assessment as **polymorphic instrument**; ROUND 2 — I redid Why/What/How/When with assessment-polymorphism lens (per-template scoring rubric, longitudinal aggregation semantics, multi-template UI router, `team-ssi-view.html` rename consideration) and 6 new open questions; reuse everything; rethink, but be grounded"); ROUND 3 (final) — locked direction = **Beta = single-template (SSI); future families plug into seams already in code; no new collections / fields / FE renames until a 2nd family is actually scoped**. **Deliverable shipped**: extended [ASSESSMENT_LIFECYCLE.md](../KARVIA_STRATEGY/2-TECHNICAL/ASSESSMENT_LIFECYCLE.md) (T2-ARC-023, the canonical assessment reference doc) with a new §"Plug-in seams (multi-template forward-compat)" section enumerating **7 seams** with file:line refs to current code — (1) Template definition at [AssessmentTemplate.js `dimension_configs`](../server/models/AssessmentTemplate.js); (2) Scoring framework at [UnifiedSSIScoringService.js:739 `toCanonicalShape`](../server/services/UnifiedSSIScoringService.js#L739) (the A20260512-17 pattern); (3) Rollup dispatch at [OnboardingProgressService.js:103 `SCORING_DISPATCH`](../server/services/OnboardingProgressService.js#L103) (the A20260512-08 pattern); ✅ honor canonical-shape contract (seam 2); ✅ extend SCORING_DISPATCH (seam 3). **Q8 entry under "Locked design decisions" amended** to reflect as-shipped reuse of `Assessment.assessment_type` per E.3 / A20260512-08 instead of the originally-spec'd new `AssessmentTemplate.scoring_type` field (per `feedback_reuse_max`) — the stale doc entry was correctable to-the-letter as shipped. **Doc-creation choice followed `feedback_extend_before_wrap`**: extended existing T2-ARC-023 instead of creating a sibling `ASSESSMENT_PLUGIN_CONTRACT.md` — the new content layered naturally under the existing "Architectural posture" heading. **Two Beta-polish audit IDs minted in [AUDIT_TRACKER.md](../KARVIA_STRATEGY/3-DELIVERY/2-QA-AND-TESTING/AUDIT_TRACKER.md) under new section "OPEN — Assessment Plug-In Seams + Beta Polish 2026-05-13"**: (a) **A20260513-05** (BETA-POLISH-A, HIGH) Employee "View Results" → blank `team-ssi-view.html?tab=my-results&assessment=<id>`; ~10-20 LoC FE rewire expected, stay under SSI scope. extend `notifyTransition()` onto history-only path per the explicit comment hook at [LifecycleTransitionService.js:50](../server/services/LifecycleTransitionService.js#L50) "if a S26 subscriber needs them, the call site can be added on the history-only path with the same payload shape"). **Doc shipped**: NEW [KARVIA_STRATEGY/1-PRODUCT/ACTIVATION_PLAYBOOK.md](../KARVIA_STRATEGY/1-PRODUCT/ACTIVATION_PLAYBOOK.md) (T1-PRD-ACTIVATION-PLAYBOOK, DRAFT, ~260 LoC) — companion to PERSONA_STAGE_OWNERSHIP_MATRIX (canonical static ownership; Non-pending statuses (expired/cancelled/bounced) explicitly excluded from "outstanding" predicate so bounced addresses don't trap forever. (2) [server/services/LifecycleTransitionService.js](../server/services/LifecycleTransitionService.js) — new `notifyTransition` branch matching `(service='company_stage', historyActor='system:aggregate_complete')` → `dispatchAggregateCompleteEmail(payload)`. Dispatcher: APP_URL fail-fast, single `generateAggregateNarrative()` call up front so all recipients share the same AI body, three send paths — BO via `primary_contact.email` (fallback to BUSINESS_OWNER User in tenant), Coach gated on `isConsultingMode()`, team enumerate. Per-recipient try/catch so one bad email doesn't block the others. NEW helpers `validateAggregateNarrative`/`staticAggregateNarrative`/`generateAggregateNarrative` — AI invariant gate (length 80-2000, no debug tokens TODO/FIXME/XXX/console.log/[DEBUG], mirrors PX-5.3 shape but tuned for OUTPUT). On AI fail OR `FEATURE_OPENAI_ENABLED!=true` → deterministic static narrative derived from rollup scores. Internals exposed for tests: `_dispatchAggregateCompleteEmail`/`_generateAggregateNarrative`/`_validateAggregateNarrative`/`_staticAggregateNarrative`. (3) [server/services/mailjetService.js](../server/services/mailjetService.js) — `MailjetService.prototype.sendAssessmentAggregateCompleteEmail` + `getAssessmentAggregateCompleteTemplate` mirroring B.1/B.2/B.4 pattern. Persona-conditional Subject branched on `recipient_kind` ∈ {'bo','coach','team'}. Team persona one-liner inside infoBox per A20260506-07 — MANAGER/EXECUTIVE/EMPLOYEE each gets distinct "What this means" copy in same template. (4) [client/pages/team-ssi-view.html](../client/pages/team-ssi-view.html) — `<div id="next-step-container">` + `/js/next-step.js` loaded BEFORE controller (~5 LoC). (5) [client/pages/scripts/team-ssi-view.js](../client/pages/scripts/team-ssi-view.js) — `fetchCompanyDetails()` sibling-reads `result.company \|\| result.data` into `this.companyDoc` (captures `stage_history` + `stage` for the gate; doesn't disturb pre-existing `result.data.name` line per `feedback_minimal_change_grounding`). New `maybeRenderAggregateCompleteNextStep()` method bails when marker absent OR `stage==='active'` (handoff #4 owns surface). Role recipes verbatim from playbook lines 195-198: BUSINESS_OWNER/EXECUTIVE → "Generate Objectives →" / `/pages/objective-wizard.html` / non-dismissible; CONSULTANT consulting-mode → "Open Workspace →" / `client-workspace.html?client=<id>#tab=objectives` / non-dismissible; MANAGER → explicit early-return; EMPLOYEE → render only when `?source=email` (informational, dismissible, no CTA). Telemetry keys `next_step.aggregate_complete.{bo,coach,employee}`. (6) NEW [scripts/test-sprint26-B.3-aggregate-complete-dispatcher.js](../scripts/test-sprint26-B.3-aggregate-complete-dispatcher.js) **92/92 ✓** — 14 static-grep sections + 10 live-fire scenarios via require.cache stubs. STATIC covers all 6 surfaces, AI invariant gate constants/logic, playbook canon references quoted, FE wiring + role recipe gates. LIVE-FIRE: consulting + 2 team → 4 sends with narrative ride-along (proves Q-B3-2 (a) one-LLM-call invariant); self-serve + 1 team → 2 sends no Coach; wrong historyActor → 0 sends; APP_URL unset → 0 sends; static fallback narrative shape; `validateAggregateNarrative` 7 invariant assertions; OnboardingProgressService — predicate satisfied/outstanding>0/already-marked/zero-completion scenarios. **AI invariant gate design**: B3_NARRATIVE_MIN=80 / B3_NARRATIVE_MAX=2000 / B3_DEBUG_TOKENS=['TODO','FIXME','XXX','console.log(','[DEBUG]']. **Test-pattern lesson learned**: initial test used `clearStubs()`-then-reinstall between scenarios; on the SECOND scenario Node re-required `LifecycleTransitionService.js` → which re-imported the real `Objective` model file → Mongoose threw `OverwriteModelError`. B.4's test pattern (which I'd initially copied from) actually stubs `Objective` upfront so the real model file never executes. I rewrote to `setStub`-based pattern where all Mongoose model stubs are installed ONCE upfront and dispatcher-deps are OVERWRITTEN between scenarios (LTS module reused; only its inner dynamic-required deps change). Hit + fixed in one iteration. **Full S26 regression sweep — 18/18 scripts green, 644/644 ✓**: 18+16+17+29+59+9+23+54+68+92+62+24+43+32+26+34+29+29 (added B.3's 92/92; B.2/B.4/B.1/B.6/C.5/E-workstream all unchanged). **Memory rules**: `feedback_reuse_max` (zero new helpers outside dispatcher itself; reused appendHistoryOnly + cohortDetection + Mailjet.prototype + brandedHeaderTemplate + LLMGateway.complete + existing GET /api/companies/:id response + `<NextStep>` component verbatim), `feedback_extend_before_wrap` (extended OnboardingProgressService body with new try/catch block vs spinning a separate aggregate-evaluator; extended notifyTransition with one new `if` branch; extended MailjetService.prototype with one new method+template; extended team-ssi-view.js with one new method), `feedback_minimal_change_grounding` (no new endpoint, no new schema field, no new model, no new collection — marker rides on existing stage_history; pre-existing `result.data.name` read bug left untouched, sibling-read added defensively for B.3 only), `feedback_no_destructive_without_greenlight` (4 Qs pre-edit, all greenlit), `feedback_quote_the_canon` (playbook role recipe lines 195-198 quoted in regression test [12]; matrix line 62 trigger semantics drove Q-B3-1 resolution; A20260506-07 referenced for persona-conditional contract), `feedback_audit_governance` (A20260506-07 + A20260513-01 3-stage flips with citations + AUDIT_TRACKER summary tally update), `feedback_test_fixture_validation` (caught the Mongoose model-recompile fixture issue in scenario [16] before the test went into the regression suite; rewrote to setStub pattern). **Quality reflection: 9/10**. Surfaced 8 bugs/issues including: (1) My Clients tile SSI aggregation broken post-A20260512-17 canonical reshape (`$avg('$ssi_result.overall')` averaging objects → NaN → blank `—` on consultant landing page); (2) SSI ring overlapping edit pencil at tile top-right; (3) Risk badge "URGENT" with no breakdown of which trigger fired; (4) Objectives CONSTRAINT KPI reading per-Assessment latest taker (taker-dependent — changes per respondent); (5) DRIVERS label colliding with playbook "DRIVER" ball-position verb per `feedback_quote_the_canon`; (6) VELOCITY KPI reading `Move.countDocuments` which is always 0 until S27 (dead KPI); (7) Plan TODAY + CATCH-UP cards same dead-Move-dependency issue; (8) Plan THIS WEEK uncovered-KR math relied on fragile `quarterly.objective_id.key_results` populated-doc dependency. **10 holistic decisions surfaced** (D1-D10); user replied "agree with all your recommendations". **Pre-edit Q gate**: 5 micro-Qs (Q1-Q5) surfaced + greenlit all (a) defaults — tuple migration single-write contract / `bucketByLifecycle` extension / canonical lowest dim (no sub-dim drill) / coverage skip 0-KR objectives / governance-first row mint. **5 surfaces touched** ([server/routes/consultant.js](../server/routes/consultant.js) BE: tuple shape change + 4 caller migration + new BALL_POSITION_VIEWS/furthestBallPosition helpers + CONSTRAINT canonical-source swap + Move.countDocuments queries dropped; [client/pages/scripts/client-workspace.js](../client/pages/scripts/client-workspace.js) FE: 3 KPI strip rebuilds — Summary 4-card, Objectives 4-card, Plan 3-card + 3 defensive tuple reads; [client/pages/scripts/my-clients.js](../client/pages/scripts/my-clients.js) FE: defensive tuple read on tile; [client/css/my-clients.css](../client/css/my-clients.css) FE: `.mc-tile-header padding-right: 36px` for pencil clearance). **5 NEW regression scripts — 118/118 ✓** total (13+35+13+36+21). Each commit standalone (independent slice + own regression). **Memory rules**: `feedback_why_what_how_when` (holistic Why-first walkthrough before any code touch — user explicitly asked for brainstorm), `feedback_minimal_change_grounding` (no new endpoints, no schema changes, no new models — all changes inside existing handlers/render functions), `feedback_reuse_max` (CONSTRAINT swap reused canonical `Company.assessment_scores` already-fetched by other endpoints; ball-position derivation reused `bucketByLifecycle` output; defensive tuple read patterns reused across 3 FE consumers; existing $ifNull pattern at line 1269 reused as template for portfolio-summary), `feedback_extend_before_wrap` (added BALL_POSITION_VIEWS + furthestBallPosition near existing bucketByLifecycle helper rather than spinning a separate module; extended computeRiskStatus to tuple shape rather than wrapping/parallel function), `feedback_no_destructive_without_greenlight` (D1-D10 holistic + Q1-Q5 pre-edit all surfaced before first edit; defensive tuple reads (`typeof === 'object'` guards) + `k.ownership || k.drivers` fallback on FE for cached-payload safety even after BE response shape changes), `feedback_quote_the_canon` (5 BALL_POSITION_VIEWS labels + descriptions quoted verbatim from ACTIVATION_PLAYBOOK §"The ball-position lens"; DRIVERS→OWNERSHIP rename specifically to eliminate the prior verb-collision drift), `feedback_audit_governance` (5 audit IDs minted in PLAN state before slice 1 per Q5 (a); each flipped to 💻→✅ as slice landed; commit messages cite audit IDs; AUDIT_TRACKER + handoff history both updated for each slice in lockstep), `feedback_test_fixture_validation` (one regression test syntax error caught + fixed in one iteration during slice 4 — IIFE wrapping the callback was syntactically wrong; rewrote as direct boolean). **Quality reflection: 9/10**. (2) **Bundle-Fix 2+4 impact analysis (40 min)** — paused before deletion per `feedback_no_destructive_without_greenlight`; grep + 3 parallel Read passes surfaced drift the HOTFIX_DASHBOARD_NOTES.md plan did NOT anticipate: text-button (`openReassignOwnerDialog`) called canonical `/api/teams/eligible-owners` (S26 C.5 / S27 F.6), but avatar (`showOwnerDropdown` → `getTeamMembers`) called LEGACY `/api/users` — completely different endpoints, different role gates, different UIs. Naive deletion of text button would have lost the only UI entrypoint to the canonical endpoint. **5 drift findings (D1-D5)** captured in impact-analysis report: D1 gate divergence (loose `canAssignOwner` vs strict `state.canReassign`), D2 consultant cross-tenant `?company_id=` missing on avatar path, D3 shape mismatch (eligible-owners returns `name` not `first_name+last_name`), D4 `teamMembersCache` tenant-stale across consultant switches, D5 empty-state copy alarming. **Recommended merged fix** (delete text-button + migrate avatar to canonical endpoint in ONE stroke) — user authorized "go" → executed 8 steps A-H in 2 atomic edits in `client/pages/scripts/objectives.js` (~−180 deletes / +110 adds): step A-C delete `openReassignOwnerDialog` (~65 LoC) + `window.openReassignOwnerDialog` export + text-button rendering; step D refactor `getTeamMembers` → `fetchEligibleOwners` against canonical endpoint with `ConsultantPageMode.detect()` propagation; step E refactor `showOwnerDropdown` with empty-state copy upgrade ("No other eligible owners — invite a Manager or Executive first") + pass selected user object into callback (no cache lookup); step F refactor `assignObjectiveOwner` to accept user-object as 2nd arg; step G tighten `renderObjectiveOwnerBadge` gate to use `state.canReassign` when available + hide on cancelled/archived; step H drop `teamMembersCache` variable + cache-find at L2040. Verified clean via `node -c` syntax + grep confirmed zero stale `openReassignOwnerDialog` / `teamMembersCache` / `getTeamMembers` references. (3) **Bundle-Fix 5 root-cause find** — paused again per `feedback_canonical_engine_grounding` for impact analysis; HOTFIX doc had blamed FE destructuring at `client-workspace.js:636-637` but parallel grep + Read of `client/js/common.js:282-292` revealed the REAL root cause: `function escapeHtml(text) { if (!text) return '';` swallows zero (because `!0 === true`). User-observed cascade: `esc(0)` → `""` → blank `% progress` in meta row + blank `at_risk`/`behind` counts in KR mix row + `style="width:${esc(0)}%"` = `"width:%"` invalid CSS → browser fallback to container width → appearance of "fully red bar at 100%" on fresh objectives. **480 esc() call sites across 45 files; 10 files have file-local helpers with same bug** — chose canonical-only fix per `feedback_minimal_change_grounding` (1-line `client/js/common.js:282` change: `text === null || text === undefined` + NaN/Infinity guard for `String(NaN) === "NaN"` rendering); captured 9 file-local helpers as `RT-ESCAPE-HTML-DUPLICATES` for a separate sweep. **Unit-test verification in isolation** via `node -e` extracted function eval: `escapeHtml(0)='0'` ✓ `escapeHtml(false)='false'` ✓ `escapeHtml(null)=''` ✓ `escapeHtml(undefined)=''` ✓ `escapeHtml(NaN)=''` ✓ `escapeHtml(Infinity)=''` ✓ `escapeHtml("")=""` ✓ `escapeHtml("hello")="hello"` ✓ `escapeHtml("<script>")="&lt;script&gt;"` ✓ `escapeHtml(42)="42"` ✓ — **10/10 pass**. (4) **Commit + push + deploy verification loop** — user pushed via GitHub (commit `13342f6` on origin/development), reported issue still existing on karvia-business-1 with screenshot showing 5 Move cards with "R" avatar; rigorous diagnosis: (a) `git fetch origin` confirmed commit landed; (b) `curl -sI` on static assets confirmed `common.js` Last-Modified 23:47:54 UTC (commit time); (c) `curl -s` on `common.js` body confirmed new "2026-06-02 — Treat null/undefined as empty" comment present; (d) `curl -s` on `objectives.js` body confirmed `grep -c "openReassignOwnerDialog" = 0` (was 4+ before fix); (e) user shared Network tab screenshot showing JWT in Authorization header → decoded JWT locally with `node -e Buffer.from(...).toString('base64url')` → `user_id: 6a15de1f3677ac4aaa8910b6`, `company_id: 6a15de1f3677ac4aaa8910b4`, `role: CONSULTANT`; (f) **direct API probe with user's JWT** via `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer ..."` on `/api/moves/dashboard-summary` returned `moves.length: 0` — definitively proved server-side fix WAS active and filtering correctly; (g) diagnosed: user's browser cached the pre-deploy response in private-window session memory (Firefox private windows DO cache within-session); recommended close-and-reopen private window. User confirmed "yeah its fixed" on fresh session. (5) **RT-TASK-MGMT-CASCADE-AUDIT mint** — user surfaced upstream concern from karvia-business-1 walk: task completion not propagating to planning page / objectives page / consultant My Clients tab. User methodology directive (quoted verbatim, aligned with `feedback_state_parsimony`): _"every time we think of adding a flag or field in the backend, we should estimate if that info would be required in any other place, or adding this will reduce the computing across any other places — for example here task progress can be updated in the task, which is actually used to upstream up until objective, which even is viewed by consultant"_. Structured RT as **4 phases**: P1 Canonical Cascade Matrix (~3-4h design-only: entities × fields × persisted-derived × computed-on-read × cascade-triggers × state-parsimony-verdict × staleness-tolerance), P2 State-Parsimony Audit Pass (~4-6h: drop persisted copies that can be derived, extract Service methods for N-place recomputes, add event/trigger seams), P3 Reassignment Model Across Every Scenario (~6-8h: 16+ scenarios = 4 cascade levels × 4 actor roles; gap audit: KR / WeeklyGoal / Task owner reassign endpoints), P4 Detailed Regression Test Suite (~6-8h: BST scenarios covering happy path + uncomplete + soft-delete + manual vs auto-update of KR.current_value + multi-tenant isolation + consultant cross-tenant + race conditions + reassign-mid-flight + cancelled-objective cascade; performance gates N=100/500/1000). **Beta-criticality flag**: current breakage erodes circular-economy promise (`Σ tasks_completed ÷ Σ nudges_sent` per CLAUDE.md); recommends carving hot-fix slice (~2-4h, likely missing `Task.completed → KR.current_value` hook) from P1 to ship BEFORE Beta with full audit deferred post-Beta.
